552	# I-deepfix-001 U10 (Codex P1): the rule name the tier classifier fires for BOTH the
553	# OpenAlex retraction flag AND a title-only retraction / withdrawal marker
554	# (tier_classifier._detect_retraction_marker -> matched_rules "R0_retracted").
555	_TIER_RETRACTION_RULE = "R0_retracted"
556	
557	
558	def _row_is_retracted(oa: Any, tier_result: Any) -> bool:
559	    """True iff the grounded evidence row must carry ``is_retracted=True`` for the
560	    generator retraction grounding gate (``retraction_gate.partition_pool`` keys on
561	    exactly this row flag).
562	
563	    Fires when EITHER:
564	      * the OpenAlex enrichment flags the paper retracted (the legacy leg), OR
565	      * the tier classifier fired its ``R0_retracted`` rule — which ALSO catches a
566	        TITLE-ONLY retraction / withdrawal marker whose OpenAlex flag was UNSET
567	        (I-deepfix-001 U10: a retracted paper re-deposited on a preprint host). Before
568	        this fix the row was flagged only from the OpenAlex leg, so a title-marker
569	        retracted paper entered the grounding pool with a non-empty ``direct_quote``.
570	
571	    Placement / credibility metadata only. The FROZEN faithfulness engine (strict_verify /
572	    NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED — this feeds the SEPARATE
573	    clinical-safety retraction gate, not any faithfulness check. Fail-open: on any missing
574	    signal the row is NOT flagged (a source with no retraction info grounds normally)."""
575	    if _retraction_is_truthy(oa, "is_retracted"):
576	        return True
577	    matched = getattr(tier_result, "matched_rules", None) or ()
578	    return _TIER_RETRACTION_RULE in matched

581	@dataclass
582	class LiveRetrievalResult:
583	    classified_sources: list[CorpusSource]
584	    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]
585	    total_candidates_pre_filter: int
586	    candidates_kept_by_scope: int
587	    candidates_kept_by_offtopic: int
588	    candidates_fetched: int
589	    candidates_failed_fetch: int
590	    api_calls: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
591	    notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
592	    # #958 (S2): fail-loud corpus-truncation signal. True when the post-fetch
593	    # loop budget (PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET) broke mid-corpus, leaving later
594	    # candidates unclassified. Defaults keep existing constructors valid.
595	    corpus_truncated: bool = False
596	    candidates_total: int = 0
597	    candidates_processed: int = 0
598	    # I-deepfix-001 item 4 (#1344): RETRIEVAL-PHASE wall telemetry (render-PASS
599	    # partial, DISTINCT from corpus_truncated). True iff the per-question retrieval
600	    # deadline (PG_RETRIEVAL_WALL_SECONDS) tripped in EITHER the search fan-out or
601	    # the post-fetch classify loop, so the run handed off a PARTIAL corpus and
602	    # rendered (it did NOT die on a bare timeout, and it is NOT gated out the way
603	    # corpus_truncated is). `retrieval_queries_skipped` = planned sub-queries never
604	    # fired; `retrieval_candidates_unclassified` = fetched bodies not reached before
605	    # the wall. Defaults keep existing constructors valid + byte-identical OFF path
606	    # (the wall never trips within budget => all False/0).
607	    retrieval_wall_hit: bool = False
608	    retrieval_queries_skipped: int = 0
609	    retrieval_candidates_unclassified: int = 0
610	    # I-deepfix-001 P1-2 (#1344): True iff the B4 semantic relevance scorer was
611	    # REQUESTED (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1) but the semantic embedder was
612	    # unavailable so it fell back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut (live_retriever
613	    # ~4423). The winner-firing gate reads this as a SECOND W6/B4 dark signal
614	    # (independent of evidence_selector._SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE): a requested
615	    # semantic winner that silently degraded to lexical is NOT firing. Default
616	    # False keeps existing constructors valid + the byte-identical OFF path (the
617	    # gate is never requested when the B4 gate is OFF).
618	    semantic_relevance_fell_back: bool = False
619	    # I-ready-017 #1134: journal_only metadata sidecar, keyed by canonical URL.
620	    # Populated ONLY on the journal_only ON path (None = OFF = byte-identical).
621	    # Carries the per-source journal-article signals (openalex pub_type /
622	    # source_type / is_peer_reviewed / is_retracted / doi / venue) that the
623	    # citeability predicate needs; merged across retrieval stages by the sweep.
624	    journal_metadata_sidecar: dict[str, Any] | None = None
625	    # I-fetch-003 (#1175 / AC3): NEW retrieval-throughput diagnostics, emitted
626	    # as SIBLING fields (NOT folded into api_calls: dict[str, int] — that
627	    # contract stays unwidened). None when the parallel-fetch path did not run
628	    # (serial fallback or no candidates). fetch_success_rate = usable / (usable
629	    # + failed); parallel_completion_rate = completed-in-deadline / submitted.
630	    fetch_success_rate: float | None = None
631	    parallel_completion_rate: float | None = None
632	    fetch_workers: int | None = None
633	    distinct_hosts: int | None = None
634	    # I-ready-017 Task 2a (#1204): ADDITIVE source-funnel telemetry (read-only).
635	    # Persists existing-but-unsaved retrieval counts so the ~90% pre-fetch
636	    # source loss is MEASURABLE on a fresh run. NONE of these change what is
637	    # discovered/filtered/fetched/selected — they only mirror locals already
638	    # computed inside run_live_retrieval.
639	    #   prefetch_offtopic: the off-topic filter's kept/rejected/threshold. None
640	    #     when the filter is disabled or only seeds are present (seed_only) —
641	    #     honestly absent, never a faked count.
642	    #   drop_reasons: per-reason aggregate of the in-run _trace_drop calls
643	    #     (offtopic / rerank_not_selected / fetch_failed / content_starved). The
644	    #     four statically-known reasons are pre-seeded to 0 so the schema is
645	    #     stable; counts derive locally (not from the pathB trace contextvar,
646	    #     which is empty on a normal sweep).
647	    prefetch_offtopic: dict[str, Any] | None = None
648	    drop_reasons: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
649	    # B4 (b1b10 redesign, I-arch-005 Phase-2/3): relevance-ORDER + fetch-budget
650	    # selection telemetry. Populated ONLY on the B4 ON path
651	    # (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1); None when OFF => byte-identical. §-1.3
652	    # DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3): the floor no longer hard-drops below-floor
653	    # candidates — it orders them to the tail and DISCLOSES the demote conversion
654	    # (`demoted_below_floor` / `demoted_fetched_to_fill` / `demoted_tail`). Records
655	    # both the above-floor unfetched tail and the below-floor demoted tail (the fetch
656	    # BUDGET could not afford) so the recall cost is measurable, never dropped-and-
657	    # forgotten. A plain dict (RelevanceGateResult.to_dict()) so the manifest
658	    # write is a no-op getattr with a None default for pre-B4 callers.
659	    relevance_gate: dict[str, Any] | None = None
660	    # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311): content-relevance judge telemetry — DISTINCT from the
661	    # B4 `relevance_gate` above so the two never conflate. Populated ONLY on the
662	    # W2 ON path (PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=1); None when OFF => byte-identical.
663	    # Carries the per-passage DEMOTE dispositions (kept-at-low-weight, never a
664	    # drop list — §-1.3 weight-not-filter).
665	    content_relevance: dict[str, Any] | None = None
666	    #   extraction_finding_rows: the EXTRACTION-stage finding-row count captured
667	    #     at run_live_retrieval RETURN time (== len(evidence_rows) here), frozen
668	    #     as an int. Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: run_one_query MUTATES
669	    #     retrieval.evidence_rows AFTER this returns (expansion append, deepener/
670	    #     agentic reassign), so reading len(evidence_rows) at manifest-write time
671	    #     reports the POST-expansion total, not the extraction yield. This frozen
672	    #     int is the stable fetched->finding extraction count.
673	    extraction_finding_rows: int = 0
674	    # I-deepfix-001 D5 (#1344): the HONEST machine-readable credibility-tiering batch
675	    # status produced by classify_sources_llm_tiering (a TieringBatchResult.tiering_status:
676	    # {tiering_mode, llm_success_count, rules_floor_count, fallback_count, error_count,
677	    # total}). Plumbed through to the durable manifest disclosure so the diced preflight's
678	    # D5 gate can assert `tiering_mode != 'rules_floor_degraded'` on a FRESH run — i.e. that
679	    # GLM credibility-tiering actually fired and the batch did not silently collapse to the
680	    # deterministic rules-floor. Default {} = LLM-tiering OFF, or no deferred sources to tier
681	    # (the W5 block never ran) => byte-identical OFF path. PURE telemetry: credibility stays a
682	    # WEIGHT (no drop, no abort — §-1.3); the faithfulness engine is untouched.
683	    credibility_tiering_status: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
684	    # I-deepfix-001 (wall/tiering-abort fix, #1344) P1a: fetch-SUBWALL disclosure —
685	    # DISTINCT from `retrieval_wall_hit`. True iff the content-fetch batch was bounded to a
686	    # FRACTION of the remaining wall (PG_RETRIEVAL_FETCH_WALL_FRACTION < 1.0) AND that cutoff
687	    # actually fired (some tasks timed out or were never dispatched before the fetch cutoff).
688	    # Those candidates flow on as ordinary `fetch_failed` and are DISCLOSED here as a wall
689	    # cutoff (§-1.3 disclose-don't-drop), never silently dropped. Kept SEPARATE from
690	    # `retrieval_wall_hit` because the FULL retrieval wall did NOT trip — every planned
691	    # sub-query fired and the classify/W5 loop kept its reserved slice — so flipping
692	    # `retrieval_wall_hit` would emit a misleading "N sub-queries unfired" note. Defaults keep
693	    # existing constructors byte-identical + the OFF path (fraction=1.0 => no subwall =>
694	    # False/0). `*_count` mirror parallel_report.timeout_count / not_dispatched_count.
695	    fetch_subwall_hit: bool = False
696	    fetch_subwall_timeout_count: int = 0
697	    fetch_subwall_not_dispatched_count: int = 0

730	# I-meta-007b (#meta-007): per-tool utilization tracer. Record-only + fail-safe.
731	# The tracer NEVER changes retrieval behavior or return values; any tracer error
732	# (import failure, None tracer, disk error) is swallowed here so a telemetry bug
733	# can never break retrieval. Lazy import keeps the dependency one-directional.
734	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
735	def _trace_tool(
736	    tool_name: str,
737	    target: str = "",
738	    status: str = "ok",
739	    latency_ms: float = 0.0,
740	    bytes_sent: int = 0,
741	    bytes_received: int = 0,
742	    backend_used: str = "",
743	    error: str = "",
744	    **metadata: Any,
745	) -> None:
746	    try:
747	        from src.polaris_graph.telemetry.tool_tracer import (
748	            get_tool_tracer,
749	            tool_tracker_enabled,
750	        )
751	        # I-meta-007b P2b: gate on PG_ENABLE_TOOL_TRACKER (default ON) so a
752	        # direct caller OUTSIDE run_one_query (which never ran the per-query
753	        # reset/bind) cannot append to a stale ON singleton when tracking is
754	        # disabled. When OFF this is a pure no-op.
755	        if not tool_tracker_enabled():
756	            return
757	        get_tool_tracer().record(
758	            tool_name=tool_name,
759	            target=target,
760	            status=status,
761	            latency_ms=latency_ms,
762	            bytes_sent=bytes_sent,
763	            bytes_received=bytes_received,
764	            backend_used=backend_used,
765	            error=error,
766	            **metadata,
767	        )
768	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must never break retrieval
769	        pass

772	def _resp_content_len(resp: Any) -> int:
773	    """Best-effort byte length of an httpx response body for telemetry.
774	
775	    Returns 0 when the response object lacks a measurable ``content`` (e.g. a
776	    lightweight test double). NEVER raises — record-only telemetry must not
777	    depend on the response's concrete shape.
778	    """
779	    try:
780	        content = getattr(resp, "content", None)
781	        return len(content) if content else 0
782	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must never break retrieval
783	        return 0

791	# FX-17 (#1126): Serper `num` is a PAGE size (provider max ~20); large result sets need the `page`
792	# param (pagination). The old code silently floored `num` to 20 with no warning and never paginated.
793	_SERPER_PAGE_MAX = 20
794	
795	
796	def _serper_fetch_page(
797	    query: str, per_page: int, page: int, headers: dict[str, str]
798	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], bool, float, int, str]:
799	    """FX-17 (#1126): fetch ONE Serper page. Returns (items, ok, latency_ms, resp_bytes, error).
800	    Byte-identical to the legacy single call when page==1 (no `page` key in the payload)."""
801	    payload: dict[str, Any] = {"q": query, "num": per_page}
802	    if page > 1:
803	        payload["page"] = page
804	    _t0 = time.time()
805	    try:
806	        with httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT) as c:
807	            r = c.post(SERPER_ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=headers)
808	        _latency_ms = (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0
809	        if r.status_code != 200:
810	            return [], False, _latency_ms, _resp_content_len(r), f"HTTP {r.status_code}"
811	        organic = (r.json().get("organic", []) or [])
812	        items = [
813	            {"url": it.get("link", ""), "title": it.get("title", ""),
814	             "snippet": it.get("snippet", ""), "source": "serper"}
815	            for it in organic
816	        ]
817	        return items, True, _latency_ms, _resp_content_len(r), ""
818	    except Exception as exc:
819	        return [], False, (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0, 0, str(exc)
820	
821	
822	def _serper_search(
823	    query: str, num: int = 10, api_calls: dict[str, int] | None = None
824	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
825	    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY", "").strip()
826	    if not api_key:
827	        logger.warning("[live_retriever] SERPER_API_KEY missing — skipping Serper")
828	        return []
829	    # I-safety-002b (#925) PR-2: record that the Path-B-required backend was actually
830	    # invoked (key present + call attempted). assert_post_run rejects a run where a
831	    # required backend was never tried. Lazy + best-effort; no-op when gate is off.
832	    try:
833	        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
834	        _pathb.record_retrieval_attempt("serper")
835	    except Exception:
836	        pass
837	    headers = {"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"}
838	    per_page = max(1, min(num, _SERPER_PAGE_MAX))
839	    # FX-17 (#1126): make the silent clamp VISIBLE — the inert PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER=100 used to floor
840	    # to 20 with no signal. Surface it loudly so the requested-vs-served gap stops lying.
841	    _clamped = num > _SERPER_PAGE_MAX
842	    if _clamped:
843	        logger.warning(
844	            "[live_retriever] Serper num=%d exceeds the page max %d — clamping per-page to %d and "
845	            "paginating to the PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY budget.", num, _SERPER_PAGE_MAX, per_page,
846	        )

848	    # the legacy single call; the benchmark slate raises PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY. Page count is
849	    # bounded by PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES (small) and early-stops when a page returns < per_page.

# would HARD-SKIP a concurrent caller of a LIVE URL whose reserved fetches will
# SUCCEED — wrongly suppressing a fetch that could succeed, which violates the
# §-1.3 "never suppress a fetch that could succeed" / successes-uncapped
# constraint. A SUCCESS never counts toward the cap. Only FAILED fetches count;
# once a URL has FAILED `cap` times it is cached and permanently short-circuited.
#
# Concurrency honesty: in the real call topology the refetch loop is SERIAL (the
# `for anchor in primary_trial_anchors` grounding loop), so a dead URL is fetched
# exactly `cap` times. Under hypothetical TRUE concurrency a dead URL could see a
# small bounded OVERSHOOT (callers already in-flight when the cap-th failure
# populates the cache) — that is an acceptable perf trade-off; it NEVER over-
# suppresses (the iter-3 constraint), and it still kills the ~5.5x storm. A
# threading lock guards every read/write of the shared dicts.
#
# The cache is process-local + bounded; it is a PERFORMANCE de-dup, NOT a
# faithfulness gate — it only stops repeating a fetch that ALREADY failed cap
# times, never suppresses a fetch that could succeed. Default cap 2; env-tunable.
_REFETCH_CAP_DEFAULT = 2
# Per-URL count of SETTLED failed attempts (a success never counts toward the cap).
_refetch_failures: dict[str, int] = {}
# URLs whose SETTLED failures have reached the cap (the permanent-skip set).
_refetch_negative_cache: set[str] = set()
_refetch_cache_lock = threading.Lock()


def _refetch_cap() -> int:
    """F15: per-URL refetch cap, read at CALL time (LAW VI — env-overridable per
    run). <= 0 disables the cap (a failing URL may be retried without bound — the
    legacy pre-F15 behavior)."""
    try:
        return int(os.getenv("PG_REFETCH_PER_URL_CAP", str(_REFETCH_CAP_DEFAULT)))
    except ValueError:
        return _REFETCH_CAP_DEFAULT


def reset_refetch_cache() -> None:
    """F15: clear the per-URL failure counters + negative cache. Called at the
    start of each retrieval run (and by tests) so the bounded cache does not leak
    across independent runs/vectors in a long-lived process."""
    with _refetch_cache_lock:
        _refetch_failures.clear()
        _refetch_negative_cache.clear()


def _refetch_try_acquire(url: str) -> bool:
    """F15: True => caller MAY fetch; False => skip because the URL has FAILED the
    cap number of times (it is in the negative cache). Gates ONLY on settled
    failures — NEVER on in-flight/concurrent reservations — so a concurrent caller
    of a LIVE URL is never wrongly skipped (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1: never
    suppress a fetch that could succeed). Thread-safe (single lock)."""
    if not url:
        return True
    with _refetch_cache_lock:
        return url not in _refetch_negative_cache


def _refetch_settle(url: str, *, succeeded: bool) -> None:
    """F15: settle the outcome of a fetch. A SUCCESS is a no-op (a live URL never
    counts toward the cap and may be re-grounded by many sections). A FAILURE
    increments the settled-failure count and, at the cap, caches the URL so
    further requests short-circuit. Thread-safe (single lock)."""
    if not url or succeeded:
        return
    _cap = _refetch_cap()
    with _refetch_cache_lock:
        n = _refetch_failures.get(url, 0) + 1
        _refetch_failures[url] = n
        if _cap > 0 and n >= _cap:
            _refetch_negative_cache.add(url)


def _refetch_should_skip(url: str) -> bool:
    """F15: True iff this URL has SETTLED-failed to the cap (permanently cached).
    Equivalent to `not _refetch_try_acquire(url)`; kept as a pure read for tests /
    diagnostics. Thread-safe."""
    if not url:
        return False
    with _refetch_cache_lock:
        return url in _refetch_negative_cache


def _refetch_record_failure(url: str) -> None:
    """F15: record a failure for a URL acquired OUTSIDE the try/settle protocol
    (e.g. a test or a non-reserved path). Increments the settled-failure count and
    caches at the cap. Thread-safe."""
    if not url:
        return
    _cap = _refetch_cap()
    with _refetch_cache_lock:
        n = _refetch_failures.get(url, 0) + 1
        _refetch_failures[url] = n
        if _cap > 0 and n >= _cap:
            _refetch_negative_cache.add(url)


def _credibility_redesign_enabled() -> bool:
    """F15/F30: master switch for the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE redesign (§-1.3 /
    I-arch-002 #1246), mirroring ``evidence_selector._credibility_redesign_enabled``.
    Default OFF — when ``PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN`` is unset, the
    content-starved / landing-page rows stay on the legacy HARD-DROP path so the
    OFF path is byte-identical. When ON, those rows are DOWN-WEIGHTED (kept in the
    pool at low weight) instead of dropped."""
    return os.environ.get("PG_SWEEP_CREDIBILITY_REDESIGN", "").strip().lower() in (
        "1", "true", "yes", "on",
    )


# F15/F30: the low retrieval weight stamped on a down-weighted (content-starved
# or landing-page) row so the composition layer can rank it last while still
# carrying it (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER). A small positive float so it never ties
# with a real full-text row but is never zero (zero would let a falsy `or`
# launder it back to a default). Read at CALL time (Codex diff-gate P2 iter-2 —
# env-overridable per run, not frozen at import). LAW VI — no magic number.
_DOWN_WEIGHT_RETRIEVAL_DEFAULT = 0.05


def _down_weight_retrieval() -> float:
    try:
        return float(os.getenv("PG_DOWN_WEIGHT_RETRIEVAL", str(_DOWN_WEIGHT_RETRIEVAL_DEFAULT)))
    except (TypeError, ValueError):
        return _DOWN_WEIGHT_RETRIEVAL_DEFAULT


def _row_degraded_flags(tier_result: Any, *, recovered: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """I-deepfix-001 (Codex P1 #2 — no-laundering): the degraded-flag keys to merge onto a
    grounded evidence row so the corpus-adequacy grounded-content count
    (``corpus_adequacy_gate.count_grounded_rows``) EXCLUDES a source the tier layer already
    flagged ``tier_result.fetch_degraded`` (a short KNOWN-scholarly-venue / DOI stub — it keeps
    its venue-authority TIER weight but must not count as grounded content, so venue authority can
    never launder a contentless stub into "adequate"; see ``tier_classifier`` B17).

    The tier classifier sets ``fetch_degraded`` but the live row previously copied only
    ``tier_result.tier.value``, so a stub that is NOT otherwise ``content_starved`` /
    ``landing_page`` / ``fetch_failed`` still counted as grounded. Returns ``{'fetch_degraded':
    True}`` only when the tier layer flagged it; ``{}`` otherwise, so a normal (non-degraded) row is
    BYTE-IDENTICAL.

    ``recovered`` (BUG-B02/B04 forced-refetch): the tier ``fetch_degraded`` is a
    CLASSIFICATION-TIME signal computed on the ORIGINAL stub body. When a forced Zyte re-fetch later
    UPGRADED the stub to full text, the row proceeds as a NORMAL full-text row and that stale flag
    MUST NOT be propagated (the existing design contract — "recovered rows have degraded flags never
    set"). Returns ``{}`` when ``recovered`` regardless of the stale tier flag.

    Faithfulness-NEUTRAL: adds a LABEL only — never a claim/span/citation, never the faithfulness
    engine, and the row keeps its tier WEIGHT (§-1.3 WEIGHT-AND-LABEL, never a drop)."""
    if recovered:
        return {}
    if getattr(tier_result, "fetch_degraded", False):
        return {"fetch_degraded": True}
    return {}


# I-deepfix-001 U10 (Codex P1): the rule name the tier classifier fires for BOTH the
# OpenAlex retraction flag AND a title-only retraction / withdrawal marker
# (tier_classifier._detect_retraction_marker -> matched_rules "R0_retracted").
_TIER_RETRACTION_RULE = "R0_retracted"


def _row_is_retracted(oa: Any, tier_result: Any) -> bool:
    """True iff the grounded evidence row must carry ``is_retracted=True`` for the
    generator retraction grounding gate (``retraction_gate.partition_pool`` keys on
    exactly this row flag).

    Fires when EITHER:
      * the OpenAlex enrichment flags the paper retracted (the legacy leg), OR
      * the tier classifier fired its ``R0_retracted`` rule — which ALSO catches a
        TITLE-ONLY retraction / withdrawal marker whose OpenAlex flag was UNSET
        (I-deepfix-001 U10: a retracted paper re-deposited on a preprint host). Before
        this fix the row was flagged only from the OpenAlex leg, so a title-marker
        retracted paper entered the grounding pool with a non-empty ``direct_quote``.

    Placement / credibility metadata only. The FROZEN faithfulness engine (strict_verify /
    NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance span-grounding) is UNTOUCHED — this feeds the SEPARATE
    clinical-safety retraction gate, not any faithfulness check. Fail-open: on any missing
    signal the row is NOT flagged (a source with no retraction info grounds normally)."""
    if _retraction_is_truthy(oa, "is_retracted"):
        return True
    matched = getattr(tier_result, "matched_rules", None) or ()
    return _TIER_RETRACTION_RULE in matched


@dataclass
class LiveRetrievalResult:
    classified_sources: list[CorpusSource]
    evidence_rows: list[dict[str, Any]]
    total_candidates_pre_filter: int
    candidates_kept_by_scope: int
    candidates_kept_by_offtopic: int
    candidates_fetched: int
    candidates_failed_fetch: int
    api_calls: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
    notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    # #958 (S2): fail-loud corpus-truncation signal. True when the post-fetch
    # loop budget (PG_POST_FETCH_LOOP_BUDGET) broke mid-corpus, leaving later
    # candidates unclassified. Defaults keep existing constructors valid.
    corpus_truncated: bool = False
    candidates_total: int = 0
    candidates_processed: int = 0
    # I-deepfix-001 item 4 (#1344): RETRIEVAL-PHASE wall telemetry (render-PASS
    # partial, DISTINCT from corpus_truncated). True iff the per-question retrieval
    # deadline (PG_RETRIEVAL_WALL_SECONDS) tripped in EITHER the search fan-out or
    # the post-fetch classify loop, so the run handed off a PARTIAL corpus and
    # rendered (it did NOT die on a bare timeout, and it is NOT gated out the way
    # corpus_truncated is). `retrieval_queries_skipped` = planned sub-queries never
    # fired; `retrieval_candidates_unclassified` = fetched bodies not reached before
    # the wall. Defaults keep existing constructors valid + byte-identical OFF path
    # (the wall never trips within budget => all False/0).
    retrieval_wall_hit: bool = False
    retrieval_queries_skipped: int = 0
    retrieval_candidates_unclassified: int = 0
    # I-deepfix-001 P1-2 (#1344): True iff the B4 semantic relevance scorer was
    # REQUESTED (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1) but the semantic embedder was
    # unavailable so it fell back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut (live_retriever
    # ~4423). The winner-firing gate reads this as a SECOND W6/B4 dark signal
    # (independent of evidence_selector._SEMANTIC_EMBEDDER_CACHE): a requested
    # semantic winner that silently degraded to lexical is NOT firing. Default
    # False keeps existing constructors valid + the byte-identical OFF path (the
    # gate is never requested when the B4 gate is OFF).
    semantic_relevance_fell_back: bool = False
    # I-ready-017 #1134: journal_only metadata sidecar, keyed by canonical URL.
    # Populated ONLY on the journal_only ON path (None = OFF = byte-identical).
    # Carries the per-source journal-article signals (openalex pub_type /
    # source_type / is_peer_reviewed / is_retracted / doi / venue) that the
    # citeability predicate needs; merged across retrieval stages by the sweep.
    journal_metadata_sidecar: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    # I-fetch-003 (#1175 / AC3): NEW retrieval-throughput diagnostics, emitted
    # as SIBLING fields (NOT folded into api_calls: dict[str, int] — that
    # contract stays unwidened). None when the parallel-fetch path did not run
    # (serial fallback or no candidates). fetch_success_rate = usable / (usable
    # + failed); parallel_completion_rate = completed-in-deadline / submitted.
    fetch_success_rate: float | None = None
    parallel_completion_rate: float | None = None
    fetch_workers: int | None = None
    distinct_hosts: int | None = None
    # I-ready-017 Task 2a (#1204): ADDITIVE source-funnel telemetry (read-only).
    # Persists existing-but-unsaved retrieval counts so the ~90% pre-fetch
    # source loss is MEASURABLE on a fresh run. NONE of these change what is
    # discovered/filtered/fetched/selected — they only mirror locals already
    # computed inside run_live_retrieval.
    #   prefetch_offtopic: the off-topic filter's kept/rejected/threshold. None
    #     when the filter is disabled or only seeds are present (seed_only) —
    #     honestly absent, never a faked count.
    #   drop_reasons: per-reason aggregate of the in-run _trace_drop calls
    #     (offtopic / rerank_not_selected / fetch_failed / content_starved). The
    #     four statically-known reasons are pre-seeded to 0 so the schema is
    #     stable; counts derive locally (not from the pathB trace contextvar,
    #     which is empty on a normal sweep).
    prefetch_offtopic: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    drop_reasons: dict[str, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
    # B4 (b1b10 redesign, I-arch-005 Phase-2/3): relevance-ORDER + fetch-budget
    # selection telemetry. Populated ONLY on the B4 ON path
    # (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1); None when OFF => byte-identical. §-1.3
    # DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3): the floor no longer hard-drops below-floor
    # candidates — it orders them to the tail and DISCLOSES the demote conversion
    # (`demoted_below_floor` / `demoted_fetched_to_fill` / `demoted_tail`). Records
    # both the above-floor unfetched tail and the below-floor demoted tail (the fetch
    # BUDGET could not afford) so the recall cost is measurable, never dropped-and-
    # forgotten. A plain dict (RelevanceGateResult.to_dict()) so the manifest
    # write is a no-op getattr with a None default for pre-B4 callers.
    relevance_gate: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311): content-relevance judge telemetry — DISTINCT from the
    # B4 `relevance_gate` above so the two never conflate. Populated ONLY on the
    # W2 ON path (PG_CONTENT_RELEVANCE_JUDGE=1); None when OFF => byte-identical.
    # Carries the per-passage DEMOTE dispositions (kept-at-low-weight, never a
    # drop list — §-1.3 weight-not-filter).
    content_relevance: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    #   extraction_finding_rows: the EXTRACTION-stage finding-row count captured
    #     at run_live_retrieval RETURN time (== len(evidence_rows) here), frozen
    #     as an int. Codex diff-gate iter-1 P1: run_one_query MUTATES
    #     retrieval.evidence_rows AFTER this returns (expansion append, deepener/
    #     agentic reassign), so reading len(evidence_rows) at manifest-write time
    #     reports the POST-expansion total, not the extraction yield. This frozen
    #     int is the stable fetched->finding extraction count.
    extraction_finding_rows: int = 0
    # I-deepfix-001 D5 (#1344): the HONEST machine-readable credibility-tiering batch
    # status produced by classify_sources_llm_tiering (a TieringBatchResult.tiering_status:
    # {tiering_mode, llm_success_count, rules_floor_count, fallback_count, error_count,
    # total}). Plumbed through to the durable manifest disclosure so the diced preflight's
    # D5 gate can assert `tiering_mode != 'rules_floor_degraded'` on a FRESH run — i.e. that
    # GLM credibility-tiering actually fired and the batch did not silently collapse to the
    # deterministic rules-floor. Default {} = LLM-tiering OFF, or no deferred sources to tier
    # (the W5 block never ran) => byte-identical OFF path. PURE telemetry: credibility stays a
    # WEIGHT (no drop, no abort — §-1.3); the faithfulness engine is untouched.
    credibility_tiering_status: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    # I-deepfix-001 (wall/tiering-abort fix, #1344) P1a: fetch-SUBWALL disclosure —
    # DISTINCT from `retrieval_wall_hit`. True iff the content-fetch batch was bounded to a
    # FRACTION of the remaining wall (PG_RETRIEVAL_FETCH_WALL_FRACTION < 1.0) AND that cutoff
    # actually fired (some tasks timed out or were never dispatched before the fetch cutoff).
    # Those candidates flow on as ordinary `fetch_failed` and are DISCLOSED here as a wall
    # cutoff (§-1.3 disclose-don't-drop), never silently dropped. Kept SEPARATE from
    # `retrieval_wall_hit` because the FULL retrieval wall did NOT trip — every planned
    # sub-query fired and the classify/W5 loop kept its reserved slice — so flipping
    # `retrieval_wall_hit` would emit a misleading "N sub-queries unfired" note. Defaults keep
    # existing constructors byte-identical + the OFF path (fraction=1.0 => no subwall =>
    # False/0). `*_count` mirror parallel_report.timeout_count / not_dispatched_count.
    fetch_subwall_hit: bool = False
    fetch_subwall_timeout_count: int = 0
    fetch_subwall_not_dispatched_count: int = 0


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# I-meta-002-q1d (#945): per-call retrieval-trace helpers. Best-effort, lazy-import, no-op when the
# trace is not started — PURELY OBSERVATIONAL (the retrieval/verify chokepoint is never altered).
# Mirrors the existing record_retrieval_attempt idiom (lazy import + swallow any error).
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _trace_query(backend: str, query: str, urls: list[str]) -> None:
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
        _pathb.record_retrieval_query(backend, query, urls)
    except Exception:
        pass


def _trace_kept(url: str, backend: str) -> None:
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
        _pathb.record_retrieval_kept(url, backend)
    except Exception:
        pass


def _trace_drop(url: str, reason: str) -> None:
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
        _pathb.record_retrieval_drop(url, reason)
    except Exception:
        pass


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# I-meta-007b (#meta-007): per-tool utilization tracer. Record-only + fail-safe.
# The tracer NEVER changes retrieval behavior or return values; any tracer error
# (import failure, None tracer, disk error) is swallowed here so a telemetry bug
# can never break retrieval. Lazy import keeps the dependency one-directional.
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
def _trace_tool(
    tool_name: str,
    target: str = "",
    status: str = "ok",
    latency_ms: float = 0.0,
    bytes_sent: int = 0,
    bytes_received: int = 0,
    backend_used: str = "",
    error: str = "",
    **metadata: Any,
) -> None:
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.telemetry.tool_tracer import (
            get_tool_tracer,
            tool_tracker_enabled,
        )
        # I-meta-007b P2b: gate on PG_ENABLE_TOOL_TRACKER (default ON) so a
        # direct caller OUTSIDE run_one_query (which never ran the per-query
        # reset/bind) cannot append to a stale ON singleton when tracking is
        # disabled. When OFF this is a pure no-op.
        if not tool_tracker_enabled():
            return
        get_tool_tracer().record(
            tool_name=tool_name,
            target=target,
            status=status,
            latency_ms=latency_ms,
            bytes_sent=bytes_sent,
            bytes_received=bytes_received,
            backend_used=backend_used,
            error=error,
            **metadata,
        )
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must never break retrieval
        pass


def _resp_content_len(resp: Any) -> int:
    """Best-effort byte length of an httpx response body for telemetry.

    Returns 0 when the response object lacks a measurable ``content`` (e.g. a
    lightweight test double). NEVER raises — record-only telemetry must not
    depend on the response's concrete shape.
    """
    try:
        content = getattr(resp, "content", None)
        return len(content) if content else 0
    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — telemetry must never break retrieval
        return 0


# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
# API clients
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


# FX-17 (#1126): Serper `num` is a PAGE size (provider max ~20); large result sets need the `page`
# param (pagination). The old code silently floored `num` to 20 with no warning and never paginated.
_SERPER_PAGE_MAX = 20


def _serper_fetch_page(
    query: str, per_page: int, page: int, headers: dict[str, str]
) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], bool, float, int, str]:
    """FX-17 (#1126): fetch ONE Serper page. Returns (items, ok, latency_ms, resp_bytes, error).
    Byte-identical to the legacy single call when page==1 (no `page` key in the payload)."""
    payload: dict[str, Any] = {"q": query, "num": per_page}
    if page > 1:
        payload["page"] = page
    _t0 = time.time()
    try:
        with httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT) as c:
            r = c.post(SERPER_ENDPOINT, json=payload, headers=headers)
        _latency_ms = (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0
        if r.status_code != 200:
            return [], False, _latency_ms, _resp_content_len(r), f"HTTP {r.status_code}"
        organic = (r.json().get("organic", []) or [])
        items = [
            {"url": it.get("link", ""), "title": it.get("title", ""),
             "snippet": it.get("snippet", ""), "source": "serper"}
            for it in organic
        ]
        return items, True, _latency_ms, _resp_content_len(r), ""
    except Exception as exc:
        return [], False, (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0, 0, str(exc)


def _serper_search(
    query: str, num: int = 10, api_calls: dict[str, int] | None = None
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    api_key = os.getenv("SERPER_API_KEY", "").strip()
    if not api_key:
        logger.warning("[live_retriever] SERPER_API_KEY missing — skipping Serper")
        return []
    # I-safety-002b (#925) PR-2: record that the Path-B-required backend was actually
    # invoked (key present + call attempted). assert_post_run rejects a run where a
    # required backend was never tried. Lazy + best-effort; no-op when gate is off.
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
        _pathb.record_retrieval_attempt("serper")
    except Exception:
        pass
    headers = {"X-API-KEY": api_key, "Content-Type": "application/json"}
    per_page = max(1, min(num, _SERPER_PAGE_MAX))
    # FX-17 (#1126): make the silent clamp VISIBLE — the inert PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER=100 used to floor
    # to 20 with no signal. Surface it loudly so the requested-vs-served gap stops lying.
    _clamped = num > _SERPER_PAGE_MAX
    if _clamped:
        logger.warning(
            "[live_retriever] Serper num=%d exceeds the page max %d — clamping per-page to %d and "
            "paginating to the PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY budget.", num, _SERPER_PAGE_MAX, per_page,
        )
    # FX-17 (#1126): total-URL budget across pages. DEFAULT = one page (per_page) -> byte-identical to
    # the legacy single call; the benchmark slate raises PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY. Page count is
    # bounded by PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES (small) and early-stops when a page returns < per_page.

874	    seen: set[str] = set()
875	    _pages_fetched = 0
876	    _last_latency = 0.0
877	    _last_bytes = 0
878	    _last_err = ""
879	    for _page in range(1, _n_pages + 1):
880	        items, ok, _last_latency, _last_bytes, _last_err = _serper_fetch_page(
881	            query, per_page, _page, headers
882	        )
883	        # FX-17 (#1126) iter-2: count EACH HTTP page request (a real Serper API call), not once
884	        # per query. P2 fix — api_calls['serper'] used to undercount paginated breadth.
885	        if api_calls is not None:
886	            api_calls["serper"] = api_calls.get("serper", 0) + 1
887	        if not ok:
888	            _trace_tool(
889	                "serper", target=query, status="fail", latency_ms=_last_latency,
890	                bytes_sent=len(query), bytes_received=_last_bytes,
891	                backend_used="serper_api_v1", error=_last_err, page=_page,
892	            )

894	                           _page, query[:60], _last_err)

896	        _pages_fetched += 1
897	        _new = 0

899	            u = it.get("url", "")
900	            if u and u not in seen:
901	                seen.add(u)
902	                out.append(it)

911	        elif len(items) < per_page:
912	            break  # legacy (default OFF): short page -> assume no more results.
913	    _trace_tool(
914	        "serper", target=query, status="ok" if out or not _last_err else "fail",
915	        latency_ms=_last_latency, bytes_sent=len(query), bytes_received=_last_bytes,
916	        backend_used="serper_api_v1", result_count=len(out),
917	        pages_fetched=_pages_fetched, num_requested=num, per_page=per_page,
918	        page_max=_SERPER_PAGE_MAX, clamped=_clamped, total_budget=_total,
919	        stop_on_zero_new=_stop_on_zero_new,   # BB-002 (#1171): which stop policy ran
920	    )

925	def _s2_bulk_search(query: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
926	    api_key = os.getenv("SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY", "").strip()
927	    # I-safety-002b (#925) PR-2: record S2 backend attempt (lazy, best-effort).
928	    try:
929	        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
930	        _pathb.record_retrieval_attempt("semantic_scholar")
931	    except Exception:
932	        pass
933	    headers = {}
934	    if api_key:
935	        headers["x-api-key"] = api_key
936	    params = {
937	        "query": query,
938	        "fields": "title,abstract,url,openAccessPdf,externalIds,year,venue",
939	        "limit": max(1, min(limit, 100)),
940	    }
941	    # I-meta-007b: wall-clock for the tool tracer (record-only).
942	    _t0 = time.time()
943	    try:
944	        with httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT) as c:
945	            r = c.get(S2_BULK_ENDPOINT, params=params, headers=headers)
946	        _latency_ms = (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0
947	        if r.status_code != 200:
948	            _trace_tool(
949	                "s2", target=query, status="fail", latency_ms=_latency_ms,
950	                bytes_received=_resp_content_len(r),
951	                backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", error=f"HTTP {r.status_code}",
952	            )
953	            logger.warning(
954	                "[live_retriever] S2 returned %s for %r",
955	                r.status_code, query[:60],
956	            )
957	            _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [])
958	            return []
959	        data = r.json()
960	    except Exception as exc:
961	        _trace_tool(
962	            "s2", target=query, status="fail",
963	            latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
964	            backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", error=str(exc),
965	        )
966	        logger.warning("[live_retriever] S2 exception: %s", exc)
967	        _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [])
968	        return []
969	    papers = data.get("data", []) or []
970	    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
971	    for p in papers:
972	        oa_pdf = (p.get("openAccessPdf") or {}).get("url", "")
973	        ext_ids = p.get("externalIds") or {}
974	        doi = ext_ids.get("DOI", "")
975	        # Full-scale fix (cycle 11): prefer open-access PDF, fall back
976	        # to DOI-resolved URL. Never return a bare semanticscholar.org
977	        # landing page — those are T7 abstract-only stubs that
978	        # AccessBypass deliberately skips and that inflate the T7
979	        # fraction in corpus adequacy. If neither oa_pdf nor DOI is
980	        # available, skip the paper entirely.
981	        if oa_pdf:
982	            url = oa_pdf
983	        elif doi:
984	            url = f"https://doi.org/{doi}"
985	        else:
986	            # Bare S2 landing page would be returned here; skip.
987	            continue
988	        abstract = p.get("abstract") or ""
989	        out.append({
990	            "url": url,
991	            "title": p.get("title", "") or "",
992	            "snippet": (abstract[:500] if abstract else "")[:500],
993	            "source": "s2",
994	            "s2_paper_id": p.get("paperId", ""),
995	            "doi": doi,
996	            "year": p.get("year"),
997	            "venue": p.get("venue"),
998	        })
999	    # BB-004 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): a HTTP-200 that yields ZERO usable papers
1000	    # (all lacked oa_pdf + DOI, or `data` was empty) is a DEAD-BACKEND signal — the
1001	    # legacy unconditional status="ok" reported it as success_rate=1.0 and masked the
1002	    # collapse (15 S2 calls -> 0 results on drb_90 still showed success). LAW II
1003	    # silent-downgrade fix: trace a DISTINCT "ok_zero" status + zero_yield=True so the
1004	    # discovery_funnel / run-health surfaces it LOUDLY instead of as a clean success.
1005	    # Telemetry/loudness ONLY — no evidence or verification path is touched; the
1006	    # returned list is unchanged (empty), so downstream control flow is identical.
1007	    _s2_zero_yield = len(out) == 0
1008	    _trace_tool(
1009	        "s2", target=query,
1010	        status="ok_zero" if _s2_zero_yield else "ok",
1011	        latency_ms=_latency_ms,
1012	        bytes_received=_resp_content_len(r),
1013	        backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", result_count=len(out),
1014	        zero_yield=_s2_zero_yield,
1015	    )
1016	    _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [o["url"] for o in out])
1017	    return out

1026	# BUG-M-17 (Codex full-scale pass 2): bounded body-text inspection
1027	# for article-type signals. Reads high-signal regions of fetched
1028	# content (meta tags, first 4KB, abstract/methods lead) for SR/MA,
1029	# case-report, perspective, guidance markers. Used by the classifier
1030	# as a SECONDARY signal when the title is truncated or non-diagnostic.
1031	#
1032	# NOT a full-body scan — Codex pass 2 explicitly warned:
1033	# "do not scan the entire body naively for generic terms. Add a
1034	#  bounded secondary narrative/SR signal extractor that inspects
1035	#  high-signal fetched regions only."
1036	
1037	# BUG-M-17b (Codex pass 3 BLOCKED fix): tightened to require context,
1038	# not lone keywords. Explicit publisher metadata and section headers
1039	# are trusted; lone body keywords are REJECTED because primary papers
1040	# routinely cite prior systematic reviews, meta-analyses, case series,
1041	# and guidelines in their background/methods without themselves being
1042	# that article type.
1043	
1044	# High-precision metadata patterns — trust these alone.
1045	# Each entry: (regex_pattern, attribute_order_flexible)
1046	_BODY_META_ARTICLE_TYPE_TAGS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1047	    # HTML meta citation_article_type (both attr orders)
1048	    r'<meta[^>]+citation_article_type[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
1049	    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+citation_article_type',
1050	    r'<meta[^>]+article:section[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
1051	    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+article:section',
1052	    r'<meta[^>]+prism\.section[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
1053	    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+prism\.section',
1054	    # JSON-LD
1055	    r'"articleType"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"',
1056	)
1057	
1058	# Publisher-embedded article-type HEADERS (top-of-page text).
1059	# Frontiers "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article", Nature "Article type: X" etc.
1060	_BODY_PUBLISHER_HEADERS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1061	    # Frontiers-style: "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article" (all caps, with space + "article")
1062	    r'\b(SYSTEMATIC REVIEW|META[- ]ANALYSIS|NETWORK META[- ]ANALYSIS|'
1063	    r'CASE REPORT|CASE SERIES|PERSPECTIVE|EDITORIAL|'
1064	    r'GUIDELINE|COMMENTARY|LETTER|BRIEF REPORT|OPINION) article\b',
1065	    # Nature family: "Article type: <Type>"
1066	    r'Article type:?\s*(Systematic Review|Meta-Analysis|Meta[- ]Analysis|'
1067	    r'Network Meta[- ]Analysis|Case Report|Case Series|Perspective|'
1068	    r'Editorial|Commentary|Letter|Brief Report|Opinion|Guideline)',
1069	)
1070	
1071	# Strong contextual patterns that need to co-occur to call SR_MA from body.
1072	# NEVER fire on a lone keyword — require the declared-intent-or-method shape.
1073	_BODY_SR_MA_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1074	    # "objective: to conduct a systematic review / meta-analysis"
1075	    r'objective[s]?:\s*to\s+(conduct|perform|undertake)\s+(a\s+)?(systematic review|meta[- ]analysis)',
1076	    # "we conducted a systematic review"
1077	    r'we\s+(conducted|performed|undertook)\s+(a\s+)?(systematic review|meta[- ]analysis)',
1078	    # M-17c (Codex pass 4): "this SR/MA" must be followed by a
1079	    # self-descriptive predicate within a short window. Rejects
1080	    # citation-byline shapes like "this meta-analysis by Smith et al.
1081	    # shaped the endpoint hierarchy" where SR/MA is the cited work,
1082	    # not the fetched paper.
1083	    r'this\s+(systematic review(?:\s+and\s+meta[- ]analysis)?|meta[- ]analysis)'
1084	    r'\s+(aims?|evaluates?|examines?|investigates?|assesses?|reviews?|'
1085	    r'reports?|presents?|summarizes?|synthesizes?|demonstrates?|'
1086	    r'pools?|analyses|analyzes|combines?|explores?|'
1087	    r'was\s+(conducted|performed|undertaken|registered|designed))\b',
1088	    # M-17c: "PRISMA" with contextual search/selection/extraction/flow diagram
1089	    r'PRISMA.{0,40}(search|selection|extraction|flow diagram)',
1090	    r'(search|selection|extraction|flow diagram).{0,40}PRISMA',
1091	    # M-17c (Codex pass 4): Cochrane tightened. Previously
1092	    # "cochrane (systematic )?review" fired on "A Cochrane review
1093	    # found..." citation in a primary trial. Now requires declarative
1094	    # "this Cochrane review" + self-descriptive verb, OR registration
1095	    # metadata (Cochrane Library CD-number).
1096	    r'this\s+cochrane\s+(systematic\s+)?review\s+'
1097	    r'(aims?|evaluates?|examines?|investigates?|assesses?|reports?|'
1098	    r'presents?|summarizes?|was\s+(conducted|performed|registered))\b',
1099	    # Cochrane Library registration ID (unique to Cochrane SRs)
1100	    r'cochrane\s+(database|library).*CD\d{6}',
1101	    # Conclusive meta-analytic methods signature: "pooled estimate" + "random-effects"
1102	    r'(pooled (estimate|effect|odds ratio|risk ratio|hazard ratio)).{0,200}(random[- ]effects|fixed[- ]effects)',
1103	    r'(random[- ]effects|fixed[- ]effects).{0,200}(pooled (estimate|effect|odds ratio|risk ratio|hazard ratio))',
1104	)
1105	
1106	# Case report body patterns — require declarative "we report/present"
1107	# OR the X-year-old patient opener together with PATIENT-centered framing.
1108	_BODY_CASE_REPORT_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1109	    # "we report/describe/present a/the case"  (declarative)
1110	    r'we\s+(report|describe|present)\s+(a|the)\s+case\b',
1111	    # "here we report/describe a case"
1112	    r'here\s+we\s+(report|describe|present)\s+(a|the)\s+case\b',
1113	    # "we report a X-year-old patient" (opener)
1114	    r'we\s+report\s+(a|an)\s+\d+[- ]year[- ]old',
1115	    # Patient opener at very beginning of abstract (first 300 chars)
1116	    # handled separately below — not in this tuple.
1117	)
1118	
1119	# Guideline body patterns — declarative intent.
1120	# M-17c (Codex pass 4): tightened. Previously "this (clinical
1121	# practice) guideline" and "consensus statement from" fired on
1122	# citation-byline references like "according to a consensus
1123	# statement from the Endocrine Society" in a primary trial.
1124	# Now requires the fetched article to EITHER say "this clinical
1125	# practice guideline" (fully qualified) OR be followed by a
1126	# self-descriptive verb ("provides", "recommends", "was developed",
1127	# "we developed").
1128	# M-17d (Codex pass 5): the unanchored "(clinical practice )?
1129	# guideline <verb>" still fired on dated external citations like
1130	# "The 2025 clinical practice guideline recommends...". Now requires
1131	# explicit "this" self-reference in front of the guideline noun.
1132	# M-17e (Codex pass 6): "this guideline" alone is insufficient when
1133	# the lead has already cited an external guideline (anaphoric
1134	# reference). The detector now additionally rejects matches preceded
1135	# by anaphoric-citation markers. Ambiguous verbs "summarizes" and
1136	# "describes" have been removed because they apply equally to cited
1137	# external guidelines. The bare "this clinical practice guideline"
1138	# phrase must appear at sentence start (not as object of "followed").
1139	# "updates?" added to close a recall miss.
1140	_BODY_GUIDELINE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1141	    # "This clinical practice guideline" at sentence start — must be
1142	    # subject, not object of "followed this guideline during trial"
1143	    r'(?:^|[.!?]\s+|\n\s*)this\s+clinical\s+practice\s+guideline\b',
1144	    # "This (clinical practice) guideline ... <verb>" — must be "this"
1145	    # to reject dated/external citations (M-17d). Verb list tightened
1146	    # to unambiguous self-authorship verbs (M-17e).
1147	    r'this\s+(clinical\s+practice\s+)?guideline\s+'
1148	    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|is\s+intended|'
1149	    r'presents|outlines|aims?|establishes|offers|updates?)',
1150	    # "this consensus statement ... verb" (declarative, not cited)
1151	    r'this\s+consensus\s+statement\s+'
1152	    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|outlines|presents|'
1153	    r'aims?|establishes|summarizes)',
1154	    # "Consensus statement from X: <descriptive phrase>" — structural
1155	    # form of a consensus paper, NOT "according to a consensus
1156	    # statement from". Require the consensus statement to be the
1157	    # subject of a descriptive predicate.
1158	    r'consensus\s+statement\s+from\s+(the\s+)?[A-Z][A-Za-z ]+\s+'
1159	    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|outlines|presents|aims?)',
1160	    # "Expert consensus panel/group was convened/developed..."
1161	    r'expert\s+consensus\s+(panel|group)\s+'
1162	    r'(was\s+convened|developed|provides|recommends|presents)',
1163	    # Declarative: "we developed this clinical/practical guidance"
1164	    r'we\s+(developed|provide)\s+(this\s+)?(clinical|practical)\s+guidance',
1165	)
1166	
1167	# Perspective/commentary body patterns — declarative framing, not
1168	# audience phrases alone.
1169	_BODY_PERSPECTIVE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
1170	    # "In this perspective / commentary / editorial"
1171	    r'in\s+this\s+(perspective|commentary|editorial|opinion)',
1172	    # "This perspective examines / reviews"
1173	    r'this\s+(perspective|commentary|editorial)\s+(examines|reviews|discusses|considers)',
1174	    # "offer a perspective on"
1175	    r'(offer|present|provide)\s+a\s+perspective\s+(on|for|of)',
1176	)

1179	def _classify_from_meta_keywords(captured: str) -> str:
1180	    """Map a captured metadata/header value to a tier signal. Used
1181	    for publisher-embedded explicit article-type tags (high precision).
1182	    """
1183	    c = (captured or "").lower().strip()
1184	    if not c:
1185	        return ""
1186	    if any(k in c for k in ("systematic review", "meta-analysis",
1187	                            "meta analysis", "cochrane",
1188	                            "network meta-analysis",
1189	                            "network meta analysis")):
1190	        return "SR_MA"
1191	    if "case report" in c or "case series" in c:
1192	        return "CASE_REPORT"
1193	    if "perspective" in c:
1194	        return "PERSPECTIVE"
1195	    if "guideline" in c or "consensus" in c:
1196	        return "GUIDELINE"
1197	    if "editorial" in c or "commentary" in c:
1198	        return "PERSPECTIVE"
1199	    if "letter" in c or "opinion" in c or "brief report" in c:
1200	        return "PERSPECTIVE"
1201	    return ""

1204	def _detect_article_type_from_body(raw_content: str) -> str:
1205	    """Bounded body-text inspection for article-type signal.
1206	
1207	    Returns one of: "SR_MA", "CASE_REPORT", "PERSPECTIVE", "GUIDELINE",
1208	    or "" (no signal). Inspects only the first 8KB of content to keep
1209	    cost bounded.
1210	
1211	    M-17b (Codex pass 3 BLOCKED): tightened to avoid false positives
1212	    from primary papers citing prior systematic reviews / case reports
1213	    as background. Now requires either:
1214	
1215	    (P1) explicit publisher-embedded article-type metadata
1216	         (HTML meta tag, JSON-LD articleType, Nature-style "Article
1217	         type:" header, Frontiers-style "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article"
1218	         section marker), OR
1219	    (P2) a declarative contextual body pattern that indicates the
1220	         fetched article IS the given type (not merely citing it).
1221	
1222	    Lone keywords like "systematic review" or "case report" alone
1223	    are NOT sufficient — primary trial abstracts commonly cite prior
1224	    reviews in their background, and primary-study methods often
1225	    mention excluding case series.
1226	    """
1227	    if not raw_content:
1228	        return ""
1229	    # Bound the scan window
1230	    head = raw_content[:8000]
1231	    lead = head[:4000]
1232	    lead_lower = lead.lower()
1233	
1234	    # ── Priority 1a: explicit meta / JSON-LD article-type tags
1235	    for pattern in _BODY_META_ARTICLE_TYPE_TAGS:
1236	        m = re.search(pattern, head, re.IGNORECASE)
1237	        if m and m.lastindex:
1238	            signal = _classify_from_meta_keywords(m.group(1))
1239	            if signal:
1240	                return signal
1241	
1242	    # ── Priority 1b: publisher-embedded article-type headers
1243	    # These are ALL-CAPS or structured banners at the page top
1244	    # (Frontiers "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article", Nature "Article type: X")
1245	    for pattern in _BODY_PUBLISHER_HEADERS:
1246	        m = re.search(pattern, head, re.IGNORECASE)
1247	        if m and m.lastindex:
1248	            signal = _classify_from_meta_keywords(m.group(1))
1249	            if signal:
1250	                return signal
1251	
1252	    # ── Priority 2: declarative body patterns (must co-occur with
1253	    # study-type context, not lone keywords)
1254	    for pattern in _BODY_SR_MA_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1255	        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
1256	            return "SR_MA"
1257	    for pattern in _BODY_CASE_REPORT_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1258	        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
1259	            return "CASE_REPORT"
1260	    # "X-year-old patient" opener as first significant content in abstract
1261	    # (look only in first 500 chars to avoid methods/exclusion-criteria false positives)
1262	    opener = lead_lower[:500]
1263	    if re.search(
1264	        r'^\s*(a|this)\s+\d+[- ]year[- ]old\s+(man|woman|male|female|patient)\s+(presented|was\s+admitted|with)',
1265	        opener,
1266	    ):
1267	        return "CASE_REPORT"
1268	    # M-17e (Codex pass 6): anaphoric-citation guard. If the lead
1269	    # contains a citation-style reference to an external guideline
1270	    # ("followed the 2025 ADA guideline", "according to the NICE
1271	    # guideline"), a subsequent "This guideline <verb>" is likely
1272	    # anaphoric to that external citation rather than self-declarative.
1273	    # We therefore require the guideline match to precede, not follow,
1274	    # any such external-guideline citation in the lead.
1275	    _ANAPHORIC_GUIDELINE_CITATION = re.compile(
1276	        r'(?:followed|following|according\s+to|citing|cited|per|'
1277	        r'as\s+recommended\s+by|as\s+per|based\s+on|in\s+line\s+with)'
1278	        r'\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:\d{4}\s+)?(?:[a-z]+\s+)?(?:clinical\s+practice\s+)?'
1279	        r'guidelines?\b',
1280	        re.IGNORECASE,
1281	    )
1282	    for pattern in _BODY_GUIDELINE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1283	        m = re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE)
1284	        if m:
1285	            # Reject if an external-guideline citation appears in the
1286	            # preceding 300 chars (anaphoric reference) — e.g. "followed
1287	            # the 2025 ADA guideline ... This guideline summarizes".
1288	            preceding = lead_lower[max(0, m.start() - 300): m.start()]
1289	            if _ANAPHORIC_GUIDELINE_CITATION.search(preceding):
1290	                continue
1291	            return "GUIDELINE"
1292	    for pattern in _BODY_PERSPECTIVE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
1293	        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
1294	            return "PERSPECTIVE"
1295	
1296	    return ""
