try:
        _total = max(per_page, int(os.getenv("PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY", str(per_page))))
    except ValueError:
        _total = per_page
    try:
        _max_pages = max(1, int(resolve("PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES")))
    except ValueError:
        _max_pages = 3
    _n_pages = min(_max_pages, -(-_total // per_page))  # ceil(total/per_page)

    # BB-002 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): a sub-`per_page` page-1 count is NOT a reliable
    # end-of-results signal for an OFFSET-paginated SERP API — Serper routinely returns
    # 10 organic on page 1 even when page 2 has more, so the legacy `len(items) < per_page`
    # break short-circuited before page 2 and the PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY budget was never
    # reached (de-facto 10/query ceiling — chokepoint #4). When PG_SERPER_STOP_ON_ZERO_NEW=1
    # the loop keeps offset-paging until (a) budget met, (b) a page returns 0 NEW (post-dedup)
    # items, or (c) PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES — the only RELIABLE end-of-results signals. DEFAULT OFF
    # = byte-identical (the legacy short-page break is preserved). Discovery-breadth only; every
    # new URL flows through the same fetch -> strict_verify -> 4-role chokepoint unchanged.
    _stop_on_zero_new = resolve("PG_SERPER_STOP_ON_ZERO_NEW").strip() in (
        "1", "true", "True",
    )

    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    _pages_fetched = 0
    _last_latency = 0.0
    _last_bytes = 0
    _last_err = ""
    for _page in range(1, _n_pages + 1):
        items, ok, _last_latency, _last_bytes, _last_err = _serper_fetch_page(
            query, per_page, _page, headers
        )
        # FX-17 (#1126) iter-2: count EACH HTTP page request (a real Serper API call), not once
        # per query. P2 fix — api_calls['serper'] used to undercount paginated breadth.
        if api_calls is not None:
            api_calls["serper"] = api_calls.get("serper", 0) + 1
        if not ok:
            _trace_tool(
                "serper", target=query, status="fail", latency_ms=_last_latency,
                bytes_sent=len(query), bytes_received=_last_bytes,
                backend_used="serper_api_v1", error=_last_err, page=_page,
            )
            logger.warning("[live_retriever] Serper page %d failed for %r: %s",
                           _page, query[:60], _last_err)
            break  # fail-open: keep pages already accumulated; do not discard.
        _pages_fetched += 1
        _new = 0
        for it in items:
            u = it.get("url", "")
            if u and u not in seen:
                seen.add(u)
                out.append(it)
                _new += 1
        if len(out) >= _total:
            break  # budget met.
        if _stop_on_zero_new:
            # BB-002 (#1171): keep paging past a short page; stop only when a page
            # added 0 NEW (post-dedup) URLs — the reliable end-of-results signal.
            if _new == 0:
                break
        elif len(items) < per_page:
            break  # legacy (default OFF): short page -> assume no more results.
    _trace_tool(
        "serper", target=query, status="ok" if out or not _last_err else "fail",
        latency_ms=_last_latency, bytes_sent=len(query), bytes_received=_last_bytes,
        backend_used="serper_api_v1", result_count=len(out),
        pages_fetched=_pages_fetched, num_requested=num, per_page=per_page,
        page_max=_SERPER_PAGE_MAX, clamped=_clamped, total_budget=_total,
        stop_on_zero_new=_stop_on_zero_new,   # BB-002 (#1171): which stop policy ran
    )
    _trace_query("serper", query, [o["url"] for o in out])
    return out


def _s2_bulk_search(query: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    api_key = os.getenv("SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY", "").strip()
    # I-safety-002b (#925) PR-2: record S2 backend attempt (lazy, best-effort).
    try:
        from src.polaris_graph.benchmark import pathB_capture as _pathb
        _pathb.record_retrieval_attempt("semantic_scholar")
    except Exception:
        pass
    headers = {}
    if api_key:
        headers["x-api-key"] = api_key
    params = {
        "query": query,
        "fields": "title,abstract,url,openAccessPdf,externalIds,year,venue",
        "limit": max(1, min(limit, 100)),
    }
    # I-meta-007b: wall-clock for the tool tracer (record-only).
    _t0 = time.time()
    try:
        with httpx.Client(timeout=DEFAULT_HTTP_TIMEOUT) as c:
            r = c.get(S2_BULK_ENDPOINT, params=params, headers=headers)
        _latency_ms = (time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0
        if r.status_code != 200:
            _trace_tool(
                "s2", target=query, status="fail", latency_ms=_latency_ms,
                bytes_received=_resp_content_len(r),
                backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", error=f"HTTP {r.status_code}",
            )
            logger.warning(
                "[live_retriever] S2 returned %s for %r",
                r.status_code, query[:60],
            )
            _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [])
            return []
        data = r.json()
    except Exception as exc:
        _trace_tool(
            "s2", target=query, status="fail",
            latency_ms=(time.time() - _t0) * 1000.0,
            backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", error=str(exc),
        )
        logger.warning("[live_retriever] S2 exception: %s", exc)
        _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [])
        return []
    papers = data.get("data", []) or []
    out: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for p in papers:
        oa_pdf = (p.get("openAccessPdf") or {}).get("url", "")
        ext_ids = p.get("externalIds") or {}
        doi = ext_ids.get("DOI", "")
        # Full-scale fix (cycle 11): prefer open-access PDF, fall back
        # to DOI-resolved URL. Never return a bare semanticscholar.org
        # landing page — those are T7 abstract-only stubs that
        # AccessBypass deliberately skips and that inflate the T7
        # fraction in corpus adequacy. If neither oa_pdf nor DOI is
        # available, skip the paper entirely.
        if oa_pdf:
            url = oa_pdf
        elif doi:
            url = f"https://doi.org/{doi}"
        else:
            # Bare S2 landing page would be returned here; skip.
            continue
        abstract = p.get("abstract") or ""
        out.append({
            "url": url,
            "title": p.get("title", "") or "",
            "snippet": (abstract[:500] if abstract else "")[:500],
            "source": "s2",
            "s2_paper_id": p.get("paperId", ""),
            "doi": doi,
            "year": p.get("year"),
            "venue": p.get("venue"),
        })
    # BB-004 (I-beatboth-fix-000 #1171): a HTTP-200 that yields ZERO usable papers
    # (all lacked oa_pdf + DOI, or `data` was empty) is a DEAD-BACKEND signal — the
    # legacy unconditional status="ok" reported it as success_rate=1.0 and masked the
    # collapse (15 S2 calls -> 0 results on drb_90 still showed success). LAW II
    # silent-downgrade fix: trace a DISTINCT "ok_zero" status + zero_yield=True so the
    # discovery_funnel / run-health surfaces it LOUDLY instead of as a clean success.
    # Telemetry/loudness ONLY — no evidence or verification path is touched; the
    # returned list is unchanged (empty), so downstream control flow is identical.
    _s2_zero_yield = len(out) == 0
    _trace_tool(
        "s2", target=query,
        status="ok_zero" if _s2_zero_yield else "ok",
        latency_ms=_latency_ms,
        bytes_received=_resp_content_len(r),
        backend_used="semantic_scholar_api", result_count=len(out),
        zero_yield=_s2_zero_yield,
    )
    _trace_query("semantic_scholar", query, [o["url"] for o in out])
    return out


_DOI_FROM_URL_RE = re.compile(
    r"(10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s?#]+?)(?:[?#]|/full|/abstract|/pdf|/meta|\.html|$)",
    re.IGNORECASE,
)


# BUG-M-17 (Codex full-scale pass 2): bounded body-text inspection
# for article-type signals. Reads high-signal regions of fetched
# content (meta tags, first 4KB, abstract/methods lead) for SR/MA,
# case-report, perspective, guidance markers. Used by the classifier
# as a SECONDARY signal when the title is truncated or non-diagnostic.
#
# NOT a full-body scan — Codex pass 2 explicitly warned:
# "do not scan the entire body naively for generic terms. Add a
#  bounded secondary narrative/SR signal extractor that inspects
#  high-signal fetched regions only."

# BUG-M-17b (Codex pass 3 BLOCKED fix): tightened to require context,
# not lone keywords. Explicit publisher metadata and section headers
# are trusted; lone body keywords are REJECTED because primary papers
# routinely cite prior systematic reviews, meta-analyses, case series,
# and guidelines in their background/methods without themselves being
# that article type.

# High-precision metadata patterns — trust these alone.
# Each entry: (regex_pattern, attribute_order_flexible)
_BODY_META_ARTICLE_TYPE_TAGS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    # HTML meta citation_article_type (both attr orders)
    r'<meta[^>]+citation_article_type[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+citation_article_type',
    r'<meta[^>]+article:section[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+article:section',
    r'<meta[^>]+prism\.section[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\']',
    r'<meta[^>]+content=["\']([^"\']+)["\'][^>]+prism\.section',
    # JSON-LD
    r'"articleType"\s*:\s*"([^"]+)"',
)

# Publisher-embedded article-type HEADERS (top-of-page text).
# Frontiers "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article", Nature "Article type: X" etc.
_BODY_PUBLISHER_HEADERS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    # Frontiers-style: "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article" (all caps, with space + "article")
    r'\b(SYSTEMATIC REVIEW|META[- ]ANALYSIS|NETWORK META[- ]ANALYSIS|'
    r'CASE REPORT|CASE SERIES|PERSPECTIVE|EDITORIAL|'
    r'GUIDELINE|COMMENTARY|LETTER|BRIEF REPORT|OPINION) article\b',
    # Nature family: "Article type: <Type>"
    r'Article type:?\s*(Systematic Review|Meta-Analysis|Meta[- ]Analysis|'
    r'Network Meta[- ]Analysis|Case Report|Case Series|Perspective|'
    r'Editorial|Commentary|Letter|Brief Report|Opinion|Guideline)',
)

# Strong contextual patterns that need to co-occur to call SR_MA from body.
# NEVER fire on a lone keyword — require the declared-intent-or-method shape.
_BODY_SR_MA_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    # "objective: to conduct a systematic review / meta-analysis"
    r'objective[s]?:\s*to\s+(conduct|perform|undertake)\s+(a\s+)?(systematic review|meta[- ]analysis)',
    # "we conducted a systematic review"
    r'we\s+(conducted|performed|undertook)\s+(a\s+)?(systematic review|meta[- ]analysis)',
    # M-17c (Codex pass 4): "this SR/MA" must be followed by a
    # self-descriptive predicate within a short window. Rejects
    # citation-byline shapes like "this meta-analysis by Smith et al.
    # shaped the endpoint hierarchy" where SR/MA is the cited work,
    # not the fetched paper.
    r'this\s+(systematic review(?:\s+and\s+meta[- ]analysis)?|meta[- ]analysis)'
    r'\s+(aims?|evaluates?|examines?|investigates?|assesses?|reviews?|'
    r'reports?|presents?|summarizes?|synthesizes?|demonstrates?|'
    r'pools?|analyses|analyzes|combines?|explores?|'
    r'was\s+(conducted|performed|undertaken|registered|designed))\b',
    # M-17c: "PRISMA" with contextual search/selection/extraction/flow diagram
    r'PRISMA.{0,40}(search|selection|extraction|flow diagram)',
    r'(search|selection|extraction|flow diagram).{0,40}PRISMA',
    # M-17c (Codex pass 4): Cochrane tightened. Previously
    # "cochrane (systematic )?review" fired on "A Cochrane review
    # found..." citation in a primary trial. Now requires declarative
    # "this Cochrane review" + self-descriptive verb, OR registration
    # metadata (Cochrane Library CD-number).
    r'this\s+cochrane\s+(systematic\s+)?review\s+'
    r'(aims?|evaluates?|examines?|investigates?|assesses?|reports?|'
    r'presents?|summarizes?|was\s+(conducted|performed|registered))\b',
    # Cochrane Library registration ID (unique to Cochrane SRs)
    r'cochrane\s+(database|library).*CD\d{6}',
    # Conclusive meta-analytic methods signature: "pooled estimate" + "random-effects"
    r'(pooled (estimate|effect|odds ratio|risk ratio|hazard ratio)).{0,200}(random[- ]effects|fixed[- ]effects)',
    r'(random[- ]effects|fixed[- ]effects).{0,200}(pooled (estimate|effect|odds ratio|risk ratio|hazard ratio))',
)

# Case report body patterns — require declarative "we report/present"
# OR the X-year-old patient opener together with PATIENT-centered framing.
_BODY_CASE_REPORT_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    # "we report/describe/present a/the case"  (declarative)
    r'we\s+(report|describe|present)\s+(a|the)\s+case\b',
    # "here we report/describe a case"
    r'here\s+we\s+(report|describe|present)\s+(a|the)\s+case\b',
    # "we report a X-year-old patient" (opener)
    r'we\s+report\s+(a|an)\s+\d+[- ]year[- ]old',
    # Patient opener at very beginning of abstract (first 300 chars)
    # handled separately below — not in this tuple.
)

# Guideline body patterns — declarative intent.
# M-17c (Codex pass 4): tightened. Previously "this (clinical
# practice) guideline" and "consensus statement from" fired on
# citation-byline references like "according to a consensus
# statement from the Endocrine Society" in a primary trial.
# Now requires the fetched article to EITHER say "this clinical
# practice guideline" (fully qualified) OR be followed by a
# self-descriptive verb ("provides", "recommends", "was developed",
# "we developed").
# M-17d (Codex pass 5): the unanchored "(clinical practice )?
# guideline <verb>" still fired on dated external citations like
# "The 2025 clinical practice guideline recommends...". Now requires
# explicit "this" self-reference in front of the guideline noun.
# M-17e (Codex pass 6): "this guideline" alone is insufficient when
# the lead has already cited an external guideline (anaphoric
# reference). The detector now additionally rejects matches preceded
# by anaphoric-citation markers. Ambiguous verbs "summarizes" and
# "describes" have been removed because they apply equally to cited
# external guidelines. The bare "this clinical practice guideline"
# phrase must appear at sentence start (not as object of "followed").
# "updates?" added to close a recall miss.
_BODY_GUIDELINE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    # "This clinical practice guideline" at sentence start — must be
    # subject, not object of "followed this guideline during trial"
    r'(?:^|[.!?]\s+|\n\s*)this\s+clinical\s+practice\s+guideline\b',
    # "This (clinical practice) guideline ... <verb>" — must be "this"
    # to reject dated/external citations (M-17d). Verb list tightened
    # to unambiguous self-authorship verbs (M-17e).
    r'this\s+(clinical\s+practice\s+)?guideline\s+'
    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|is\s+intended|'
    r'presents|outlines|aims?|establishes|offers|updates?)',
    # "this consensus statement ... verb" (declarative, not cited)
    r'this\s+consensus\s+statement\s+'
    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|outlines|presents|'
    r'aims?|establishes|summarizes)',
    # "Consensus statement from X: <descriptive phrase>" — structural
    # form of a consensus paper, NOT "according to a consensus
    # statement from". Require the consensus statement to be the
    # subject of a descriptive predicate.
    r'consensus\s+statement\s+from\s+(the\s+)?[A-Z][A-Za-z ]+\s+'
    r'(provides|recommends|was\s+developed|outlines|presents|aims?)',
    # "Expert consensus panel/group was convened/developed..."
    r'expert\s+consensus\s+(panel|group)\s+'
    r'(was\s+convened|developed|provides|recommends|presents)',
    # Declarative: "we developed this clinical/practical guidance"
    r'we\s+(developed|provide)\s+(this\s+)?(clinical|practical)\s+guidance',
)

# Perspective/commentary body patterns — declarative framing, not
# audience phrases alone.
_BODY_PERSPECTIVE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS: tuple[str, ...] = (
    # "In this perspective / commentary / editorial"
    r'in\s+this\s+(perspective|commentary|editorial|opinion)',
    # "This perspective examines / reviews"
    r'this\s+(perspective|commentary|editorial)\s+(examines|reviews|discusses|considers)',
    # "offer a perspective on"
    r'(offer|present|provide)\s+a\s+perspective\s+(on|for|of)',
)


def _classify_from_meta_keywords(captured: str) -> str:
    """Map a captured metadata/header value to a tier signal. Used
    for publisher-embedded explicit article-type tags (high precision).
    """
    c = (captured or "").lower().strip()
    if not c:
        return ""
    if any(k in c for k in ("systematic review", "meta-analysis",
                            "meta analysis", "cochrane",
                            "network meta-analysis",
                            "network meta analysis")):
        return "SR_MA"
    if "case report" in c or "case series" in c:
        return "CASE_REPORT"
    if "perspective" in c:
        return "PERSPECTIVE"
    if "guideline" in c or "consensus" in c:
        return "GUIDELINE"
    if "editorial" in c or "commentary" in c:
        return "PERSPECTIVE"
    if "letter" in c or "opinion" in c or "brief report" in c:
        return "PERSPECTIVE"
    return ""


def _detect_article_type_from_body(raw_content: str) -> str:
    """Bounded body-text inspection for article-type signal.

    Returns one of: "SR_MA", "CASE_REPORT", "PERSPECTIVE", "GUIDELINE",
    or "" (no signal). Inspects only the first 8KB of content to keep
    cost bounded.

    M-17b (Codex pass 3 BLOCKED): tightened to avoid false positives
    from primary papers citing prior systematic reviews / case reports
    as background. Now requires either:

    (P1) explicit publisher-embedded article-type metadata
         (HTML meta tag, JSON-LD articleType, Nature-style "Article
         type:" header, Frontiers-style "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article"
         section marker), OR
    (P2) a declarative contextual body pattern that indicates the
         fetched article IS the given type (not merely citing it).

    Lone keywords like "systematic review" or "case report" alone
    are NOT sufficient — primary trial abstracts commonly cite prior
    reviews in their background, and primary-study methods often
    mention excluding case series.
    """
    if not raw_content:
        return ""
    # Bound the scan window
    head = raw_content[:8000]
    lead = head[:4000]
    lead_lower = lead.lower()

    # ── Priority 1a: explicit meta / JSON-LD article-type tags
    for pattern in _BODY_META_ARTICLE_TYPE_TAGS:
        m = re.search(pattern, head, re.IGNORECASE)
        if m and m.lastindex:
            signal = _classify_from_meta_keywords(m.group(1))
            if signal:
                return signal

    # ── Priority 1b: publisher-embedded article-type headers
    # These are ALL-CAPS or structured banners at the page top
    # (Frontiers "SYSTEMATIC REVIEW article", Nature "Article type: X")
    for pattern in _BODY_PUBLISHER_HEADERS:
        m = re.search(pattern, head, re.IGNORECASE)
        if m and m.lastindex:
            signal = _classify_from_meta_keywords(m.group(1))
            if signal:
                return signal

    # ── Priority 2: declarative body patterns (must co-occur with
    # study-type context, not lone keywords)
    for pattern in _BODY_SR_MA_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
            return "SR_MA"
    for pattern in _BODY_CASE_REPORT_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
            return "CASE_REPORT"
    # "X-year-old patient" opener as first significant content in abstract
    # (look only in first 500 chars to avoid methods/exclusion-criteria false positives)
    opener = lead_lower[:500]
    if re.search(
        r'^\s*(a|this)\s+\d+[- ]year[- ]old\s+(man|woman|male|female|patient)\s+(presented|was\s+admitted|with)',
        opener,
    ):
        return "CASE_REPORT"
    # M-17e (Codex pass 6): anaphoric-citation guard. If the lead
    # contains a citation-style reference to an external guideline
    # ("followed the 2025 ADA guideline", "according to the NICE
    # guideline"), a subsequent "This guideline <verb>" is likely
    # anaphoric to that external citation rather than self-declarative.
    # We therefore require the guideline match to precede, not follow,
    # any such external-guideline citation in the lead.
    _ANAPHORIC_GUIDELINE_CITATION = re.compile(
        r'(?:followed|following|according\s+to|citing|cited|per|'
        r'as\s+recommended\s+by|as\s+per|based\s+on|in\s+line\s+with)'
        r'\s+(?:the\s+)?(?:\d{4}\s+)?(?:[a-z]+\s+)?(?:clinical\s+practice\s+)?'
        r'guidelines?\b',
        re.IGNORECASE,
    )
    for pattern in _BODY_GUIDELINE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
        m = re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE)
        if m:
            # Reject if an external-guideline citation appears in the
            # preceding 300 chars (anaphoric reference) — e.g. "followed
            # the 2025 ADA guideline ... This guideline summarizes".
            preceding = lead_lower[max(0, m.start() - 300): m.start()]
            if _ANAPHORIC_GUIDELINE_CITATION.search(preceding):
                continue
            return "GUIDELINE"
    for pattern in _BODY_PERSPECTIVE_CONTEXT_PATTERNS:
        if re.search(pattern, lead_lower, re.IGNORECASE):
            return "PERSPECTIVE"

    return ""

4889	def _rerank_content_tokens(text: str) -> set[str]:
4890	    """Lowercase content-word tokens (3+ chars, stopword-filtered). Pure lexical."""
4891	    toks = re.findall(r"[A-Za-z][A-Za-z\-]{2,}", (text or "").lower())
4892	    return {t for t in toks if t not in _RERANK_STOPWORDS}
4893	
4894	
4895	def _lexical_relevance_score(candidate: "SearchCandidate", question_tokens: set[str]) -> float:
4896	    """Overlap fraction of the candidate's (title+snippet) content tokens with the
4897	    question tokens. 0.0 when either side is empty. Deterministic, no network/model."""
4898	    if not question_tokens:
4899	        return 0.0
4900	    cand_tokens = _rerank_content_tokens(getattr(candidate, "snippet_text", "") or "")
4901	    if not cand_tokens:
4902	        return 0.0
4903	    return len(cand_tokens & question_tokens) / float(len(question_tokens))
4904	
4905	
4906	# FX-15a (#1118): the injected-seed source classes that share the reserved/undroppable/unranked
4907	# lane. `primary_trial_doi` = #817 layer-4 direct primary-trial DOI seeds; `agentic_seed` =
4908	# agentic-discovered URLs; `deepener_seed` = citation-snowball deepener URLs (Codex iter-1 P1:
4909	# these are primary-trial-DERIVED but NOT direct DOI seeds, so they must not pollute
4910	# `primary_trial_doi` telemetry either). BB-006 (#1171): `storm_seed` = STORM interview-search-result
4911	# URLs harvested URL-ONLY (the synthesized STORM text is NEVER ingested). ALL are split out and
4912	# prepended unranked; FX-15b later makes the web-discovered classes droppable via the host-class
4913	# filter (telemetry-correctness here is the prerequisite).
4914	_SEED_SOURCE_LABELS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
4915	    {"primary_trial_doi", "agentic_seed", "deepener_seed", "storm_seed"}
4916	)
4917	
4918	
4919	# FX-15b (#1119): path/route markers for pages that CANNOT carry a single paper's content — pure
4920	# navigation / search-result / table-of-contents / discussion listings. Matched as lowercase
4921	# substrings of the URL. Chosen PRECISION-FIRST and EMPIRICALLY (Codex iter-1 P1 + evidence_pool.json
4922	# cross-reference on the held drb_72 trace): every one appears ONLY on listing/nav pages, never on a
4923	# page that fetched real evidence.
4924	#
4925	# DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED (Codex iter-1 P1 — these CAN bear evidence, so pre-fetch dropping is a
4926	# precision failure; the POST-fetch tier classifier + content-starvation check handle the empty ones):
4927	#   - `/conference/.../program/paper/<id>` — held S7SHZQ4n (50k chars) + S25ktKkD (30k chars) fetched
4928	#     as REAL papers; the junk 8A8RRTQY has the IDENTICAL shape, so URL cannot distinguish them.
4929	#   - `/annual-meeting/.../paper/...` — same ambiguity as conference papers.
4930	#   - conference SUPPLEMENT abstracts (`/Supplement_`) — bear abstract-level evidence; let the tier
4931	#     classifier DOWN-TIER them (it already does) rather than pre-fetch DROP them.
4932	# `/issue/` (singular) is NOT a marker (it prefixes real article paths); `/issues/` (plural TOC
4933	# listing) IS.
4934	_LOW_CONTENT_PATH_MARKERS: tuple[str, ...] = (
4935	    "/search", "/browse", "/issues/", "/forum/",
4936	    "/toc/",  # journal table-of-contents listing (e.g. /toc/jpe/current) — never a real article
4937	)
4938	
4939	
4940	def _is_low_content_host_or_page(url: str, title: str = "") -> bool:
4941	    """FX-15b (#1119): structural reject of pure NAV / SERP / table-of-contents / discussion URLs,
4942	    applied to agentic-discovered seed URLs BEFORE fetch (a cheap deterministic floor that skips a
4943	    wasted fetch on pages that cannot contain a paper). PRECISION-FIRST — must NEVER reject a page
4944	    that could fetch real evidence (conference papers, supplement abstracts, working-paper PDFs are
4945	    all KEPT and decided by the post-fetch tier classifier + content-starvation check). Pure + no
4946	    network. Returns True iff the URL is a pure listing/nav page that should be dropped.
4947	    """
4948	    if not url:
4949	        return False
4950	    u = url.lower()
4951	    if any(marker in u for marker in _LOW_CONTENT_PATH_MARKERS):
4952	        return True
4953	    # Paginated SERP / search-result listing pages (not an article).
4954	    if "search-results" in u or "per-page=" in u:
4955	        return True
4956	    return False
4957	
4958	
4959	def _rerank_and_reserve(
4960	    candidates: list["SearchCandidate"],
4961	    *,
4962	    research_question: str,
4963	    fetch_cap: int,
4964	    n_seed_injected: int,
4965	) -> list["SearchCandidate"]:
4966	    """Replace arrival-order truncation with a no-spend, no-model-load relevance rerank
4967	    that reserves at least one slot per sub-query (I-meta-002-q1d #951, Codex brief-gate
4968	    iter-1 required-changes).
4969	
4970	    Seed lane (I-bug-776 #817): primary-trial DOI seeds carry empty title/snippet, so
4971	    relevance scoring would drop them. They are SPLIT OUT by `source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS`
4972	    (FX-15a #1118: the set `{primary_trial_doi, agentic_seed}` — both injected seed classes keep
4973	    the additive/reserved lane) and prepended AFTER ranking — never ranked, never dropped, exactly
4974	    additive as before.
4975	
4976	    Reservation: group non-seeds by `query_origin`; sort each group by (-score, index);
4977	    take at most ONE reserved item per origin while capacity remains (origins with the best
4978	    candidate score reserved first when origins exceed cap); then fill the remaining slots
4979	    by global (-score, index). The long full-paragraph/anchor query is not starved.
4980	
4981	    Fail-open (never raise): on any error fall back to the previous arrival-order behavior
4982	    `candidates[:fetch_cap + n_seed_injected]`.
4983	    """
4984	    try:
4985	        seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
4986	        non_seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") not in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
4987	        if fetch_cap <= 0 or not non_seeds:
4988	            return seeds + non_seeds[:max(fetch_cap, 0)]
4989	
4990	        question_tokens = _rerank_content_tokens(research_question)
4991	        # (score, original_index, candidate) so ties + zero-overlap fall back to arrival order.
4992	        scored = [
4993	            (_lexical_relevance_score(c, question_tokens), i, c)
4994	            for i, c in enumerate(non_seeds)
4995	        ]
4996	        # Group by origin, each group sorted by (-score, index).
4997	        groups: dict[str, list[tuple[float, int, "SearchCandidate"]]] = {}
4998	        for entry in scored:
4999	            origin = getattr(entry[2], "query_origin", "") or "_unlabeled"
5000	            groups.setdefault(origin, []).append(entry)
5001	        for entries in groups.values():
5002	            entries.sort(key=lambda e: (-e[0], e[1]))
5003	
5004	        selected_idx: set[int] = set()
5005	        chosen: list[tuple[float, int, "SearchCandidate"]] = []
5006	        # Phase 1 — reserve >=1 slot per origin (origins ranked by their best candidate score),
5007	        # bounded by capacity.
5008	        origins_by_best = sorted(
5009	            groups.items(), key=lambda kv: (-kv[1][0][0], kv[1][0][1])
5010	        )
5011	        for _origin, entries in origins_by_best:
5012	            if len(chosen) >= fetch_cap:
5013	                break
5014	            top = entries[0]
5015	            chosen.append(top)
5016	            selected_idx.add(top[1])
5017	        # Phase 2 — fill remaining slots by global (-score, index).
5018	        if len(chosen) < fetch_cap:
5019	            remainder = sorted(
5020	                (e for e in scored if e[1] not in selected_idx),
5021	                key=lambda e: (-e[0], e[1]),
5022	            )
5023	            for entry in remainder:
5024	                if len(chosen) >= fetch_cap:
5025	                    break
5026	                chosen.append(entry)
5027	                selected_idx.add(entry[1])
5028	        # Emit non-seeds in original arrival order among the selected set (stable corpus).
5029	        selected_non_seeds = [c for i, c in enumerate(non_seeds) if i in selected_idx]
5030	        return seeds + selected_non_seeds
5031	    except Exception as exc:  # fail-open: never break retrieval on a ranking error
5032	        logger.warning("[live_retriever] rerank failed (%s) — arrival-order fallback", exc)
5033	        return candidates[:fetch_cap + n_seed_injected]
5034	
5035	
5036	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5037	# B4 (b1b10 redesign, I-arch-005 Phase-2/3): relevance-ORDER + fetch-BUDGET
5038	# (§-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP as of I-deepfix-001 D3 — the floor ORDERS, never filters)
5039	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5040	# THE BUG (PHASE2_3_LANE_SPECS.md LANE-RETRIEVAL): the fetch-time cut above is a
5041	# fixed top-N COUNT cut keyed on a PURE-LEXICAL relevance score
5042	# (`_lexical_relevance_score` = content-word overlap / len(question_tokens)). The
5043	# denominator scales with QUESTION LENGTH, so a long multi-part research question
5044	# (the drb_76 shape) makes an on-topic paper whose domain vocabulary does not
5045	# lexically match the question's exact words score near-zero and get cut purely
5046	# because the cap could not afford it — and the cut count was lumped into the
5047	# generic `rerank_not_selected` reason, dropped-and-forgotten.
5048	#
5049	# THE FIX (surgical, §-1.3 WEIGHT-and-CONSOLIDATE / DEMOTE-NOT-DROP): replace the
5050	# COUNT cut with a relevance ORDERING by REUSING B1's semantic relevance scorer
5051	# WHOLESALE — `evidence_selector._semantic_relevance_scores(research_question,
5052	# sub_queries, evidence_rows)`. B4 does NOT re-implement the scoring loop: it shapes
5053	# its pre-fetch candidates into the row-dicts B1's `_row_embed_text` reads, calls
5054	# B1's scorer ONCE, and uses B1's IDENTICAL `score >= floor` predicate — but as of
5055	# I-deepfix-001 D3 that predicate ORDERS + DISCLOSES, it is NOT a hard cut: above-
5056	# floor candidates rank first, below-floor candidates are DEMOTED to the tail (NOT
5057	# dropped) and survive to the fetch budget. One scorer, one relevance story across
5058	# B1+B4 — the scorer contract CANNOT drift because there is only one implementation
5059	# (Codex B4 iter-2 P1). The `fetch_cap` is then purely a COST budget on how far down
5060	# the demote-ordered list we actually fetch — and the beyond-budget tail (above-floor
5061	# relevant + below-floor demoted) is RECORDED (counts + reason + scores) instead of
5062	# silently discarded. Each fetched candidate's relevance score is carried FORWARD as
5063	# a weight onto its evidence row.
5064	#
5065	# B1's scorer returns ``None`` when the embedder is unavailable / scoring fails /
5066	# there is no usable anchor — and B4 falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical
5067	# `_rerank_and_reserve` on that ``None`` (LAW II: no silent degrade, never a silent
5068	# keep-all). That ``None`` IS the shared infra-failure signal: B4 no longer
5069	# hand-rolls a self-similarity canary (deleted in iter-3) — the canary would have
5070	# been a B4-private divergence from B1's contract, the exact drift Codex flagged.
5071	# FAIL-OPEN (I-deepfix-001, Codex wave-1 P0): `prefetch_offtopic_filter._similarity_scores`
5072	# now SIGNALS its three internal infra failures (no embedder interface / zero-norm
5073	# query / encode exception) by returning ``None`` instead of all-zeros. B1's
5074	# `_semantic_relevance_scores` propagates that as ``None`` when EVERY anchor fails,
5075	# so B4 here falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical `_rerank_and_reserve` (keeps the
5076	# candidates) on a scorer/embedder failure — it NEVER mass-drops the corpus on an
5077	# embedder hiccup. §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3): a genuinely empty/text-
5078	# less SNIPPET still scores a real 0.0, but it is now DEMOTED to the tail (kept,
5079	# fetched-to-know if the budget reaches it), NOT dropped — the only hard drop is the
5080	# downstream faithfulness engine. This is distinct from an infra failure (``None``).
5081	#
5082	# CREDIBILITY/TIER IS NEVER A DROP HERE — and as of I-deepfix-001 D3 (§-1.3
5083	# DEMOTE-NOT-DROP) the relevance floor is no longer a DROP either: it ORDERS (above-
5084	# floor first, below-floor demoted to the tail) and the fetch BUDGET is the only
5085	# bound. The faithfulness engine (strict_verify / 4-role D8 / provenance) lives in
5086	# the generator/evaluator and is UNTOUCHED: this lane only changes the pre-fetch
5087	# candidate ORDER + which fill the budget, adds an additive weight + telemetry.
5088	#
5089	# GATING: `PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE` (default ON). OFF => the legacy
5090	# `_rerank_and_reserve` count-cut runs byte-identically (the embedder is never
5091	# even imported — preserving `test_no_embedder_model_loaded`). ON + embedder
5092	# unavailable => LOUD fallback to the legacy lexical path (LAW II: no silent
5093	# degrade, never a silent keep-all).
5094	
5095	# B4 relevance floor — ONE relevance story across B1+B4 (Codex iter-1 P1.1).
5096	# The threshold is NOT a B4-private constant; it is B1's `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`
5097	# (default 0.30, `evidence_selector._DEFAULT_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`), parsed by B1's
5098	# `evidence_selector.parse_relevance_floor`. B4 and B1 use the IDENTICAL floor
5099	# against the IDENTICAL [0,1]-clamped semantic cosine. §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP
5100	# (I-deepfix-001 D3): the floor is an ORDERING + disclosure boundary, NOT a hard cut.
5101	# A cosine below this floor against EVERY anchor (question + each sub-query) marks a
5102	# candidate as below-floor — it is DEMOTED to the tail of the cosine-ordered list,
5103	# NOT removed, and survives to the fetch budget (fetched if the budget reaches it).
5104	# The floor is consumed only on the B4 ON path (PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=1); OFF
5105	# => the legacy count-cut runs byte-identically and the floor is never even read.
5106	# `parse_relevance_floor` is imported lazily inside `_relevance_gate_threshold` so
5107	# the OFF path never imports evidence_selector.

5109	def _relevance_gate_enabled() -> bool:
5110	    """Kill-switch `PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE`.
5111	
5112	    DEFAULT ON (I-deepfix-001 B1 keystone, 2026-06-28): the pre-fetch relevance
5113	    ORDER replaces the blind `_rerank_and_reserve` COUNT-cut that silently cut
5114	    on-topic candidates beyond the fetch budget. §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (D3,
5115	    2026-06-29): the floor orders (above-floor first, below-floor demoted to the
5116	    tail) but never hard-drops; the fetch BUDGET is the only bound, and the whole
5117	    unfetched tail is RECORDED (RelevanceGateResult unfetched_relevant_tail +
5118	    demoted_tail), never lost. Set `PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE=0` (or off/false/no)
5119	    to revert to the byte-identical legacy count-cut (the semantic embedder is then
5120	    never imported, preserving `test_no_embedder_model_loaded` on the OFF path)."""
5121	    raw = resolve('PG_RETRIEVAL_RELEVANCE_GATE').strip().lower()
5122	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", "")

5125	def _relevance_gate_threshold() -> float:
5126	    """Relevance floor — B1's `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR` (default 0.30), parsed by B1's
5127	    `evidence_selector.parse_relevance_floor` so B1 and B4 share ONE floor and ONE
5128	    relevance story (Codex iter-1 P1.1). §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3): a
5129	    candidate whose MAX cosine over {research_question} ∪ {sub-queries} is below this
5130	    floor is below-floor and DEMOTED to the tail of the cosine-ordered list — NOT
5131	    dropped; the fetch BUDGET decides whether it is reached. The comparison is the
5132	    IDENTICAL `score >= floor` on the IDENTICAL [0,1]-clamped cosine that B1 applies
5133	    at `_relevance_floor_selection` (`item[1] >= relevance_floor`) — used here for
5134	    ORDERING + disclosure, not as a hard cut.
5135	
5136	    FAIL LOUD on a garbage / out-of-range `PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`: `parse_relevance_floor`
5137	    raises `ValueError` (range (0.0, 1.0]) — identical to B1's behaviour — so a
5138	    misconfigured floor can never silently pass an unbounded, off-topic pool. The
5139	    import is lazy so the OFF path never imports evidence_selector."""
5140	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import parse_relevance_floor
5141	
5142	    return parse_relevance_floor(resolve('PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR'))

5145	def _relevance_fetch_all_relevant_enabled() -> bool:
5146	    """Kill-switch `PG_RELEVANCE_FETCH_ALL_RELEVANT` (default ON).
5147	
5148	    I-fetch-005 iter-2 P0 (§-1.3, Codex HIGHEST): a RELEVANT (above-floor) source must
5149	    NEVER be stranded unfetched by the fetch BUDGET. Recording an above-floor source as an
5150	    ``unfetched_relevant_tail`` is STILL a §-1.3 DROP — an unfetched source has no content,
5151	    so it can carry no weight into composition. With this ON (the fix), EVERY above-floor
5152	    source is fetched and the fetch BUDGET bounds ONLY the below-floor (truly-off-topic)
5153	    demoted fill. Set it to 0/off/false/no to revert to the pre-fix behaviour where the
5154	    budget bounds the WHOLE ordered list (above-floor beyond the budget lands in the
5155	    recorded-but-unfetched relevant tail) — an EMERGENCY rollback only; that path drops
5156	    credible relevant sources pre-fetch and must not be used in production."""
5157	    raw = resolve('PG_RELEVANCE_FETCH_ALL_RELEVANT').strip().lower()
5158	    return raw not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "disabled", "")

5161	@dataclass
5162	class RelevanceGateResult:
5163	    """B4 telemetry for the relevance-ORDER + fetch-budget selection.
5164	
5165	    Emitted on `LiveRetrievalResult.relevance_gate` ONLY on the B4 ON path (None
5166	    when OFF => byte-identical). Records the unfetched tail (the candidates the
5167	    fetch BUDGET could not afford) so the recall cost is MEASURABLE + auditable,
5168	    never dropped-and-forgotten.
5169	
5170	    §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3, 2026-06-29, Codex iter-1 P1): the
5171	    relevance floor (`PG_RELEVANCE_FLOOR`) no longer HARD-DROPS below-floor
5172	    candidates pre-fetch — that was the §-1.3-banned hard FILTER on the sweep path.
5173	    The floor now only ORDERS + DISCLOSES: above-floor candidates rank first, the
5174	    below-floor ones are DEMOTED to the tail (in relevance order). The fetch BUDGET
5175	    (a generous disclosed COST cap) is the ONLY bound; if there are fewer above-floor
5176	    than the budget, the most-relevant below-floor candidates FILL the remainder
5177	    (`demoted_fetched_to_fill`) instead of the budget going unused. Off-topic content
5178	    with no overlap still fails the faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role
5179	    D8 / provenance) downstream — the ONLY sanctioned hard drop.
5180	
5181	    Fields:
5182	      - threshold: the relevance floor used (ordering + disclosure boundary, NOT a
5183	        drop gate).
5184	      - total_scored: non-seed candidates scored (seeds bypass scoring).
5185	      - kept_on_topic: above-floor candidate count (the on-topic survivors; these
5186	        rank first). Renamed conceptually to "above-floor count" but the key is kept
5187	        for telemetry continuity.
5188	      - demoted_below_floor: below-floor candidate count — DEMOTED (kept, re-ordered
5189	        to the tail), NOT dropped. The disclosed drop->demote conversion (replaces
5190	        the old `dropped_off_topic`, which implied a hard drop that no longer occurs).
5191	      - demoted_fetched_to_fill: below-floor candidates the BUDGET reached and FETCHED
5192	        (the budget had room beyond the above-floor set) — proof the floor is no
5193	        longer a pre-fetch hard cut.
5194	      - demoted_tail: below-floor candidates left UNFETCHED in the budget tail (a COST
5195	        bound, not a relevance/credibility drop).
5196	      - fetched_budget: total candidates the fetch BUDGET kept (above-floor fetched
5197	        + demoted_fetched_to_fill).
5198	      - unfetched_relevant_tail: above-floor candidates BEYOND the budget — RELEVANT
5199	        but unfetched for COST reasons (a real resource bound, not a relevance/
5200	        credibility drop). This is the recall cost the operator must see.
5201	      - tail_score_min / tail_score_max: the relevance-score band of the WHOLE tail
5202	        (above-floor + demoted), so the operator sees the full unfetched band.
5203	      - scorer: 'semantic_v2' (B1 embedder) or 'lexical_fallback' (embedder
5204	        unavailable — LOUD degrade per LAW II).
5205	    """
5206	
5207	    threshold: float
5208	    total_scored: int = 0
5209	    kept_on_topic: int = 0
5210	    demoted_below_floor: int = 0
5211	    demoted_fetched_to_fill: int = 0
5212	    demoted_tail: int = 0
5213	    fetched_budget: int = 0
5214	    unfetched_relevant_tail: int = 0
5215	    tail_score_min: float | None = None
5216	    tail_score_max: float | None = None
5217	    scorer: str = "semantic_v2"
5218	
5219	    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
5220	        return {
5221	            "threshold": self.threshold,
5222	            "total_scored": self.total_scored,
5223	            "kept_on_topic": self.kept_on_topic,
5224	            "demoted_below_floor": self.demoted_below_floor,
5225	            "demoted_fetched_to_fill": self.demoted_fetched_to_fill,
5226	            "demoted_tail": self.demoted_tail,
5227	            "fetched_budget": self.fetched_budget,
5228	            "unfetched_relevant_tail": self.unfetched_relevant_tail,
5229	            "tail_score_min": self.tail_score_min,
5230	            "tail_score_max": self.tail_score_max,
5231	            "scorer": self.scorer,
5232	        }

5235	def _candidate_relevance_scores(
5236	    research_question: str,
5237	    sub_queries: list[str],
5238	    candidates: list["SearchCandidate"],
5239	) -> Optional[list[float]]:
5240	    """Per-candidate topical relevance, computed by REUSING B1's scorer wholesale
5241	    (`evidence_selector._semantic_relevance_scores`) — NO B4-private scoring loop,
5242	    NO B4-private canary, so the scorer contract cannot drift (Codex B4 iter-2 P1).
5243	
5244	    Each candidate is shaped into the row-dict B1's `_row_embed_text` reads. That
5245	    helper embeds the row's ``statement`` + ``direct_quote`` fields, so a
5246	    candidate's text surface (`snippet_text` = title + snippet — the SAME surface
5247	    the lexical scorer reads) is mapped onto ``statement``. B1 then returns
5248	    ``{row_index -> max cosine over {research_question} ∪ {sub_queries}}`` clamped
5249	    to [0, 1], or ``None`` (embedder unavailable / scoring failed / no anchor).
5250	
5251	    Returns a per-candidate score list aligned to ``candidates`` (in [0, 1]), or
5252	    ``None`` — the caller falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut on ``None``
5253	    (LAW II: no silent keep-all). The import is INSIDE this function so the OFF path
5254	    (which never calls it) never imports the embedder / evidence_selector."""
5255	    try:
5256	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.evidence_selector import (
5257	            _semantic_relevance_scores,
5258	        )
5259	    except Exception as exc:  # import-path failure
5260	        logger.warning(
5261	            "[live_retriever] B4 relevance scorer import failed (%s) — falling "
5262	            "back LOUDLY to the lexical count-cut.",
5263	            str(exc)[:200],
5264	        )
5265	        return None
5266	    # Shape each candidate as the row-dict B1's `_row_embed_text` reads: it embeds
5267	    # `statement` + `direct_quote`, so the candidate's title+snippet surface goes
5268	    # onto `statement` (an empty-snippet candidate therefore embeds to ~0.0, exactly
5269	    # as B1 scores a row with no text — no B4-private bypass).
5270	    rows = [
5271	        {"statement": (getattr(c, "snippet_text", "") or "")}
5272	        for c in candidates
5273	    ]
5274	    score_map = _semantic_relevance_scores(research_question, sub_queries, rows)
5275	    if score_map is None:
5276	        # B1's None = embedder unavailable / scoring failed / no usable anchor.
5277	        return None
5278	    # `_semantic_relevance_scores` keys by row index; project back to list order.
5279	    return [float(score_map.get(i, 0.0)) for i in range(len(candidates))]
5280	
5281	
5282	def _relevance_threshold_select(
5283	    candidates: list["SearchCandidate"],
5284	    *,
5285	    research_question: str,
5286	    sub_queries: list[str],
5287	    fetch_cap: int,
5288	    n_seed_injected: int,
5289	) -> tuple[list["SearchCandidate"], dict[str, float], Optional[RelevanceGateResult]]:
5290	    """B4 ON-path selection: relevance ORDER (the floor orders, never filters) +
5291	    fetch BUDGET (cost cap), using B1's semantic embedding-cosine scorer.
5292	
5293	    §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP (I-deepfix-001 D3, 2026-06-29, Codex iter-1 P1): the floor
5294	    used to HARD-DROP every below-floor candidate BEFORE the budget slice, so a
5295	    below-floor source could NEVER be fetched no matter how high the budget — the
5296	    §-1.3-banned hard FILTER on the sweep path. It now only ORDERS + DISCLOSES.
5297	
5298	    Pipeline:
5299	      1. Split seeds (`source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS`) — NEVER scored, NEVER
5300	         dropped, prepended exactly as `_rerank_and_reserve` does (seed lane
5301	         preserved: primary-trial DOI seeds carry empty title/snippet).
5302	      2. Score every non-seed by REUSING B1's `_semantic_relevance_scores` (max
5303	         cosine over {question} ∪ {sub-queries}) — no B4-private scoring loop.
5304	      3. ORDER (no drop): rank ALL scored candidates by relevance DESC — above-floor
5305	         first, below-floor DEMOTED to the tail (in relevance order). The floor
5306	         (`score >= floor`) decides ORDER + disclosure only; it NEVER removes a
5307	         candidate. Credibility/tier is NEVER consulted.
5308	      4. BUDGET (I-fetch-005 iter-2 P0, §-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER): EVERY above-floor
5309	         (RELEVANT) source is fetched UNCONDITIONALLY — the budget must never strand a
5310	         relevant source unfetched (an unfetched source has no content and can carry no
5311	         weight into composition, so recording it as a tail is STILL a §-1.3 drop). The
5312	         fetch `fetch_cap` bounds ONLY the below-floor (truly-off-topic) demoted fill: if
5313	         the budget has room beyond the WHOLE above-floor set, the most-relevant below-floor
5314	         candidates FILL it (`demoted_fetched_to_fill`); the rest are the below-floor cost
5315	         tail (`demoted_tail`), RECORDED in `RelevanceGateResult`, not silently discarded.
5316	         `unfetched_relevant_tail` is therefore structurally 0 (proof no relevant source is
5317	         stranded). `PG_RELEVANCE_FETCH_ALL_RELEVANT=0` reverts to the pre-fix budget bound.
5318	      5. Carry each kept candidate's relevance score forward as a WEIGHT
5319	         (`relevance_weight` on the returned score map, keyed by url).
5320	
5321	    Returns `(selected_candidates, url_to_relevance_weight, gate_telemetry)`.
5322	    Selected order: seeds first (arrival order), then budget-selected non-seeds in
5323	    ARRIVAL order among the selected set (a stable corpus, mirroring
5324	    `_rerank_and_reserve`'s stable-order emit). On any scorer failure returns
5325	    `(None, {}, None)` so the caller falls back LOUDLY to the legacy lexical cut.
5326	
5327	    `n_seed_injected` is accepted for SIGNATURE PARITY with `_rerank_and_reserve`
5328	    (the caller passes the same args to either path); seeds are handled here by the
5329	    `source in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS` split, so the count itself is not read.
5330	    """
5331	    seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
5332	    non_seeds = [c for c in candidates if getattr(c, "source", "") not in _SEED_SOURCE_LABELS]
5333	    threshold = _relevance_gate_threshold()
5334	
5335	    if not non_seeds:
5336	        # Only seeds present — nothing to score; budget bounds the (empty) non-seeds.
5337	        gate = RelevanceGateResult(threshold=threshold, scorer="semantic_v2")
5338	        return seeds + non_seeds[:max(fetch_cap, 0)], {}, gate
5339	
5340	    scores = _candidate_relevance_scores(research_question, sub_queries, non_seeds)
5341	    if scores is None:
5342	        # LOUD fallback: signal the caller to run the legacy lexical count-cut.
5343	        return None, {}, None  # type: ignore[return-value]
5344	
5345	    budget = max(0, int(fetch_cap))
5346	    # (score, arrival_index, candidate) for stable, deterministic ordering.
5347	    scored = list(zip(scores, range(len(non_seeds)), non_seeds))
5348	    # §-1.3 DEMOTE-NOT-DROP: the floor ORDERS, it does not filter. Partition by the
5349	    # IDENTICAL B1 predicate (`score >= floor`, exactly `item[1] >= relevance_floor`
5350	    # at B1's `_relevance_floor_selection`) but KEEP both sides. Above-floor ranks
5351	    # first (relevance DESC), then the below-floor candidates are DEMOTED to the tail
5352	    # (relevance DESC). A text-less non-seed scores 0.0 and is demoted (not dropped)
5353	    # — it SURVIVES to the budget so it can be fetched-to-know if the budget reaches
5354	    # it. The fetch BUDGET is the ONLY bound; off-topic content with no overlap fails
5355	    # the downstream faithfulness engine (the only sanctioned hard drop), never here.
5356	    above_floor = sorted((t for t in scored if t[0] >= threshold), key=lambda t: (-t[0], t[1]))
5357	    below_floor = sorted((t for t in scored if t[0] < threshold), key=lambda t: (-t[0], t[1]))
5358	    ranked = above_floor + below_floor
5359	
5360	    # BUDGET. I-fetch-005 iter-2 P0 (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-FILTER, Codex HIGHEST): the fetch
5361	    # BUDGET must NEVER strand a RELEVANT (above-floor) source unfetched. Recording an
5362	    # above-floor source as the "unfetched relevant tail" is STILL a §-1.3 DROP — an
5363	    # unfetched source has no content and cannot carry weight into composition. So EVERY
5364	    # above-floor source is fetched UNCONDITIONALLY; the budget bounds ONLY the below-floor
5365	    # (truly-off-topic) demoted fill. If the budget has room beyond the WHOLE above-floor
5366	    # set, the most-relevant below-floor candidates FILL the remainder
5367	    # (`demoted_fetched_to_fill`); the rest sit in the cost-bound below-floor tail (a
5368	    # genuine cost cap on genuinely-off-topic content, never on a relevant source). The
5369	    # kill-switch `PG_RELEVANCE_FETCH_ALL_RELEVANT=0` reverts to the pre-fix budget bound
5370	    # (emergency rollback only — that path drops relevant sources pre-fetch).
5371	    if _relevance_fetch_all_relevant_enabled():
5372	        _demoted_budget = max(0, budget - len(above_floor))
5373	        fetched = above_floor + below_floor[:_demoted_budget]
5374	        tail = below_floor[_demoted_budget:]
5375	    else:
5376	        # Pre-fix behaviour: the budget bounds the whole demote-ordered list; above-floor
5377	        # beyond the budget lands in the (recorded, unfetched) relevant tail.
5378	        fetched = ranked[:budget]
5379	        tail = ranked[budget:]
5380	
5381	    selected_idx = {t[1] for t in fetched}
5382	    # Stable corpus: emit fetched non-seeds in ARRIVAL order among the selected set.
5383	    selected_non_seeds = [c for i, c in enumerate(non_seeds) if i in selected_idx]
5384	
5385	    # Carry the relevance score forward as a weight, keyed by url (kept set only).
5386	    url_weight: dict[str, float] = {}
5387	    for score, idx, cand in fetched:
5388	        url_weight[cand.url] = float(score)
5389	
5390	    # Disclosure splits: how the below-floor DEMOTED set was handled (fetched-to-fill
5391	    # the budget vs left in the tail) + the above-floor relevant tail (cost-bound).
5392	    demoted_fetched_to_fill = sum(1 for t in fetched if t[0] < threshold)
5393	    demoted_tail = sum(1 for t in tail if t[0] < threshold)
5394	    above_floor_tail = sum(1 for t in tail if t[0] >= threshold)
5395	
5396	    tail_scores = [t[0] for t in tail]
5397	    gate = RelevanceGateResult(
5398	        threshold=threshold,
5399	        total_scored=len(non_seeds),
5400	        kept_on_topic=len(above_floor),
5401	        demoted_below_floor=len(below_floor),
5402	        demoted_fetched_to_fill=demoted_fetched_to_fill,
5403	        demoted_tail=demoted_tail,
5404	        fetched_budget=len(fetched),
5405	        unfetched_relevant_tail=above_floor_tail,
5406	        tail_score_min=(min(tail_scores) if tail_scores else None),
5407	        tail_score_max=(max(tail_scores) if tail_scores else None),
5408	        scorer="semantic_v2",
5409	    )
5410	    return seeds + selected_non_seeds, url_weight, gate
5411	
5412	
5413	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5414	# Main entry
5415	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5416	
5417	
5418	def run_live_retrieval(
5419	    *,
5420	    research_question: str,
5421	    amplified_queries: Optional[list[str]] = None,
5422	    protocol: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
5423	    max_serper: int = DEFAULT_MAX_SERPER,
5424	    max_s2: int = DEFAULT_MAX_S2,
5425	    fetch_cap: int = DEFAULT_FETCH_CAP,
5426	    enable_openalex_enrich: bool = True,
5427	    enable_prefetch_filter: bool = False,
